# Twico UI тАФ LLM usage guide (llms.txt)

Twico UI is a free, MIT-licensed **React component library**: 62 components and 23 standalone
hooks (plus the `useToast` hook), with dark mode, motion, accessibility, RTL, a density scale, and
design tokens. **Zero runtime dependencies** (peer: `react`/`react-dom` >= 18).

This file is the authoritative guide for writing application code with the **`twico-ui` npm package**.
The component and hook reference below is generated from the library's own TypeScript types and
component data, so every component, prop, and example reflects the shipped API. When you generate
code that uses Twico UI, follow the setup and conventions here, then use the per-component sections
for exact props.

> Not to be confused with `SKILL.md` / `DESIGN-SYSTEM.md`, which are for generating static HTML
> design mockups from the design-system bundle. This guide is for **real React app code**.

## Install & setup (do this once)

```bash
npm install twico-ui
```

Import the stylesheet **once** at your app root тАФ this is the only CSS you import:

```jsx
import "twico-ui/styles.css";
```

Then import components and hooks by name from the package root:

```jsx
import { Button, Input, Dialog, useColorScheme } from "twico-ui";
```

Rules that matter:

- **Do not import any per-component CSS тАФ there is none.** The single `twico-ui/styles.css` carries
  the design tokens, base reset, and self-hosted fonts; each component injects its own scoped CSS at
  runtime. Importing `twico-ui/styles.css` more than once is harmless but unnecessary.
- The package ships a `"use client"` directive, so it works in the **Next.js App Router / React
  Server Components** out of the box тАФ import it into client trees directly.
- **SSR-safe**: components never touch `window`/`document` during render.
- **Peer deps**: `react` and `react-dom` >= 18 (works with React 18 and 19).
- TypeScript types ship with the package; every component's props type is exported (e.g.
  `ButtonProps`).

## Dark mode

Dark mode is a class/attribute on the `<html>` element тАФ add `class="dark"` (or `data-theme="dark"`):

```html
<html class="dark">
```

Or manage it in React with the built-in hook (persists to `localStorage`, syncs across tabs/instances):

```jsx
import { useColorScheme, Button } from "twico-ui";

function ThemeToggle() {
  const { isDark, toggle } = useColorScheme();
  return <Button variant="ghost" onClick={toggle}>{isDark ? "Light" : "Dark"}</Button>;
}
```

Only the semantic color aliases flip in dark mode; the primitive scales stay constant.

## Theming (design tokens)

Everything is styled through CSS custom properties, so you re-skin by overriding tokens at `:root`
(or any scope) тАФ no component props or config needed:

```css
:root {
  --brand-500: #8b5cf6;   /* rebrand: remap the primary scale */
  --radius-md: 8px;        /* tighter corners everywhere */
}
```

Token families: `--color-*` (semantic: `primary`, `success`, `warning`, `danger`, `info`, `bg`,
`surface`, `text`, `border`, тАж), `--brand-*` / `--indigo-*` (primary scale, 50тАУ950), `--radius-*`,
`--shadow-*`, `--space-*`, `--text-*`, `--font-*`, `--duration-*` / `--ease-*`. The primitive color
scales are also importable as JS objects: `import { indigo, slate } from "twico-ui/colors"`.

- **Density**: set `data-density="compact"` or `"comfortable"` on any ancestor to retune control
  heights for everything inside тАФ no per-component prop.
- **RTL**: set `dir="rtl"` on any ancestor; components use logical properties and mirror automatically.

## Controlled vs uncontrolled (read before using inputs)

Every form component supports both modes:

- **Uncontrolled**: pass `defaultValue`, read changes via `onChange`.
- **Controlled**: pass `value` + `onChange` and own the state.

The `onChange` payload differs by component kind:

- **Composite controls** (Select, MultiSelect, Combobox, Slider, Rating, Tabs, DatePicker,
  DateRangePicker, ColorPicker, тАж) call **`onChange(value)`** тАФ you receive the value directly.
- **Native input controls** (Input, Textarea, and the toggle family Checkbox / Switch / Radio) call
  **`onChange(event)`** тАФ read `event.target.value` (or `event.target.checked` for the toggles).
- Currency / CurrencyField are specialized money inputs тАФ see their sections for their exact
  `value`/`onChange` shape.

```jsx
// controlled text input (native тАФ event payload)
const [email, setEmail] = useState("");
<Input value={email} onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)} placeholder="you@example.com" />

// controlled select (composite тАФ value payload)
const [role, setRole] = useState("editor");
<Select
  value={role}
  onChange={setRole}
  options={[{ value: "admin", label: "Admin" }, { value: "editor", label: "Editor" }]}
/>
```

The `Field` component is a shared label/hint/error/required wrapper for any control. The
`useControllableState` hook implements this exact controlled/uncontrolled pattern if you build your own.

## Overlays (Dialog, Drawer, Menu, Popover, CommandPalette, Tooltip)

- **Modal overlays тАФ Dialog and Drawer тАФ are driven by `open` + `onClose`:**

```jsx
const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);
<Button onClick={() => setOpen(true)}>Open</Button>
<Dialog open={open} onClose={() => setOpen(false)} title="Confirm" description="Are you sure?">
  тАжbodyтАж
</Dialog>
```

- **Anchored overlays тАФ Menu and Popover тАФ take a `trigger` node** and use `open` / `defaultOpen` +
  `onOpenChange` (uncontrolled by default тАФ just pass `trigger` and `items`).
- All portal to `document.body`, animate in **and** out, and dismiss on backdrop click / Escape;
  `prefers-reduced-motion` is respected.
- The `useDisclosure` hook is a convenient open/close state helper for driving these.

## Toasts

Wrap your app **once** in `ToastProvider`, then call the imperative `useToast` hook anywhere beneath
it. `useToast()` returns an object тАФ **destructure `toast`**:

```jsx
import { ToastProvider, useToast, Button } from "twico-ui";

function App() {
  return (
    <ToastProvider limit={4}>
      <Page />
    </ToastProvider>
  );
}

function Page() {
  const { toast } = useToast();
  // toast.success / .error / .warning / .info / .danger(title, options?)
  return <Button onClick={() => toast.success("Saved")}>Save</Button>;
}
```

## Icons

Components take icons as **React nodes** via props like `leftIcon`, `rightIcon`, `icon`, `header` тАФ
bring any icon (inline SVG, or any icon library). Icons inherit `currentColor` and the control's size.

For convenience, **`twico-ui/icons` re-exports the full [Lucide](https://lucide.dev) icon set** (the
visual language Twico UI is built on) **plus 31 curated brand icons** Lucide omits. Lucide icons need
the **optional peer** `lucide-react` (`npm install lucide-react`) and are tree-shakeable; the brand
icons (`GithubIcon`, `VercelIcon`, `FigmaIcon`, `XTwitterIcon`, тАж) are zero-dependency inline SVG
built in тАФ **no peer needed**. The core `twico-ui` package itself stays zero-dependency. All icons
are plain SVG components (no hooks), so they work in Server Components too. Names use the `*Icon`
suffix; icons take `size` (default 24) and `color` (default `currentColor`).

```jsx
import { HomeIcon, SearchIcon } from "twico-ui/icons"; // Lucide тАФ needs the optional `lucide-react` peer
import { GithubIcon } from "twico-ui/icons";           // brand тАФ no peer needed
<Button leftIcon={<HomeIcon size={16} />}>Home</Button>
<Button variant="outline" leftIcon={<GithubIcon size={16} />}>Star</Button>

// тАжor pass your own inline SVG (no peer needed):
<Button leftIcon={<svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" /* тАж */ />}>Add item</Button>
```

## Shared prop vocabulary

- `size`: `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` (default `"md"`); a few components add `"xs"`/`"full"`.
- `tone`: semantic color. Status components expose `"primary" | "neutral" | "success" | "warning" |
  "danger" | "info"`; action components (Button, IconButton) expose `"primary" | "danger"`.
- `variant`: fill style тАФ commonly `"solid" | "soft" | "outline" | "ghost"` (the exact set varies per
  component; see each section).
- `value` / `defaultValue` / `onChange`: the controlled/uncontrolled triad.
- `items` / `options`: array-driven content.
- `label` / `hint` / `error` / `required`: form labelling (or use the `Field` wrapper).

**Every component also accepts the standard DOM attributes for its root element** (`className`, `id`,
`style`, `data-*`, `aria-*`) and forwards remaining props and event handlers via `...rest`. To keep
this guide focused, the per-component "Props" lists below show only the **component-specific** props
(the generic DOM passthrough is omitted).

## How to read the reference below

For each component: a one-line summary, its import, the component-specific props as
`` `name` `` тАФ type ┬╖ *required* or default тАФ what it does, and a copy-paste example. Object-shaped
types referenced in props (e.g. `SelectOption`, `AccordionItem`, `DatatableColumn`) are illustrated
in the examples.

---

## Component reference (76 components)

### Buttons & actions

#### Button

Button is a primary action button with solid, soft, outline, and ghost variants crossed with a primary or danger tone, four sizes (xs/sm/md/lg), loading state, optional left/right icons, and a click ripple effect. Use it for primary and secondary actions throughout the UI.

`import { Button } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `variant` тАФ `"solid" | "soft" | "outline" | "ghost"` ┬╖ default `"solid"` тАФ Fill style: solid (primary CTA), soft (tinted), outline, or ghost. Cross with `tone` for color.
- `tone` тАФ `"primary" | "danger"` ┬╖ default `"primary"` тАФ Color/intent, orthogonal to variant тАФ primary by default; tone="danger" makes a destructive button in any variant.
- `size` тАФ `"xs" | "sm" | "md" | "lg"` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Adds a new "xs" size (~26px tall, smaller than "sm") to the existing union; sm/md/lg are unchanged. xs height is composed locally from space tokens (no global control-h-xs token).
- `leftIcon` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders an icon node before the label, commonly used to reinforce the action with a leading visual cue.
- `rightIcon` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders an icon node after the label, often a chevron or arrow signaling navigation or progression.
- `loading` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ When true, swaps in a spinner and disables interaction to signal an in-flight action and prevent duplicate clicks.
- `fullWidth` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Stretches the button to fill its container's width, handy for full-width CTAs and stacked mobile action buttons.
- `as` тАФ `"button" | "a"` ┬╖ default `"button"` тАФ Chooses whether to render a native "button" or an "a" anchor, letting the button act as a real link when needed.
- `children` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ The label content rendered inside the button, typically text but accepting any inline node alongside the icons.
- `href` тАФ `string` тАФ Link destination тАФ only used with as="a"; scheme-sanitized (javascript:/data:/vbscript: render without href).
- `target` тАФ `React.HTMLAttributeAnchorTarget` тАФ Anchor target тАФ only used with as="a" (e.g. "_blank").
- `rel` тАФ `string` тАФ Anchor rel тАФ only used with as="a"; pair "noopener noreferrer" with target="_blank".
- `onClick` тАФ `(e: React.MouseEvent) => void` тАФ Click handler тАФ fires on click/tap and on Enter or Space.
- `type` тАФ `"button" | "submit" | "reset"` ┬╖ default `"button"` тАФ Native button type тАФ use "submit" to submit a surrounding form.
- `disabled` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Disables the control тАФ no interaction and a dimmed appearance.
- `autoFocus` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Focus the element automatically when it mounts.

```jsx
import { Button } from "twico-ui";

const PlusIcon = () => (<svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="2" strokeLinecap="round"><path d="M12 5v14M5 12h14" /></svg>);

<Button variant="solid" size="md" onClick={() => {}}>Save changes</Button>
<Button variant="soft" leftIcon={<PlusIcon />}>Add item</Button>
<Button variant="outline">Cancel</Button>
<Button variant="ghost">Skip</Button>
<Button tone="danger" loading>DeletingтАж</Button>
```

#### IconButton

IconButton is a square or circular button that renders a single icon and always requires an aria-label for accessibility. Use it in toolbars, cards, and dense UI where an icon-only control is clearer than a labeled button.

`import { IconButton } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `icon` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Supplies the single icon node to render, such as a Lucide SVG, falling back to children when omitted.
- `variant` тАФ `"solid" | "soft" | "outline" | "ghost"` ┬╖ default `"ghost"` тАФ Fill style (same axes as Button): solid, soft, outline, or ghost; ghost by default. Cross with `tone` for color.
- `tone` тАФ `"primary" | "danger"` ┬╖ default `"primary"` тАФ Color/intent, orthogonal to variant (mirrors Button) тАФ primary by default; tone="danger" makes an icon-only destructive button in any variant.
- `size` тАФ `"xs" | "sm" | "md" | "lg"` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Adds a new "xs" size (~26px square, smaller than "sm") to the existing union; sm/md/lg are unchanged.
- `round` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Renders a fully rounded circular button when true instead of the default squared shape with rounded corners.
- `aria-label` тАФ `string` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Provides the required accessible name for this icon-only button so screen readers announce its purpose.
- `onClick` тАФ `(e: React.MouseEvent) => void` тАФ Click handler тАФ fires on click/tap and on Enter or Space.
- `type` тАФ `"button" | "submit" | "reset"` ┬╖ default `"button"` тАФ Native button type тАФ use "submit" to submit a surrounding form.
- `disabled` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Disables the control тАФ no interaction and a dimmed appearance.
- `autoFocus` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Focus the element automatically when it mounts.

```jsx
import { IconButton } from "twico-ui";

const SettingsIcon = () => (<svg width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="2" strokeLinecap="round"><path d="M4 21v-7M4 10V3M12 21v-9M12 8V3M20 21v-5M20 12V3M1 14h6M9 8h6M17 16h6" /></svg>);
const HeartIcon = () => (<svg width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="2"><path d="M20.8 4.6a5.5 5.5 0 0 0-7.8 0L12 5.7l-1.1-1.1a5.5 5.5 0 1 0-7.8 7.8L12 21l8.9-8.6a5.5 5.5 0 0 0 0-7.8z" /></svg>);
const TrashIcon = () => (<svg width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="2" strokeLinecap="round"><path d="M3 6h18M8 6V4h8v2M19 6l-1 14H6L5 6" /></svg>);

<IconButton aria-label="Settings" icon={<SettingsIcon />} />
<IconButton aria-label="Like" variant="soft" round icon={<HeartIcon />} />
<IconButton aria-label="Delete" variant="outline" icon={<TrashIcon />} />
```

### Layout

#### AppShell

Full-height application shell: a fixed sidebar beside a scrollable content area with an optional fixed topbar. The content fills the remaining width and scrolls independently, so the sidebar and header stay put.

`import { AppShell } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `sidebar` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ The sidebar element (e.g. a Sidebar), rendered full-height on the inline-start side of the shell.
- `header` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Topbar content in a fixed header above the content; a flex row with space-between, so pass a left and a right group.
- `children` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Main content; fills the remaining space and scrolls independently of the fixed sidebar and header.
- `height` тАФ `string | number` ┬╖ default `"100dvh"` тАФ Overall shell height; pass a fixed value to embed the shell inside a smaller region (e.g. a demo).
- `padded` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Pads the scrollable content region with the standard page padding; set false for edge-to-edge content.
- `sidebarOpen` тАФ `boolean` тАФ Open state of the sidebar's off-canvas drawer on mobile; providing this (or onSidebarOpenChange) makes the shell forward overlay/open/onOpenChange to the sidebar element.
- `onSidebarOpenChange` тАФ `(open: boolean) => void` тАФ Called when the sidebar drawer requests open or close; wire a Navbar's onMenuClick to () => onSidebarOpenChange(true) for the mobile menu button.

```jsx
import { AppShell, Sidebar, Button } from "twico-ui";

<AppShell
  sidebar={
    <Sidebar
      brand={<b>Acme</b>}
      items={[{ label: "Home", active: true }, { label: "Settings" }]}
    />
  }
  header={
    <>
      <b>Dashboard</b>
      <Button size="sm">New</Button>
    </>
  }
>
  <h1>Welcome back</h1>
</AppShell>
```

#### Box

Generic, token-styled box тАФ the building block for non-flex layout (padding, margin, background, border, radius, shadow) without writing CSS.

`import { Box } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `p, px, py, pt, pr, pb, pl` тАФ `number | string` тАФ Sets inner padding on the matching side(s), accepting a spacing-scale step (number) or any raw CSS length string.
- `m, mx, my, mt, mr, mb, ml` тАФ `number | string` тАФ Sets outer margin on the matching side(s), accepting a spacing-scale step (number) or any raw CSS length string.
- `bg` тАФ `"surface" | "surface-raised" | "surface-sunken" | "bg" | string` тАФ Sets the background to a surface token like "surface" or "surface-raised", or to any custom CSS color value.
- `border` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ When true, draws a 1px border using the theme's border token, useful for visually separating the box from its surroundings.
- `radius` тАФ `string` тАФ Applies a border-radius from the radius token scale by suffix (e.g. "lg") to round the box's corners.
- `shadow` тАФ `string` тАФ Applies an elevation box-shadow from the shadow token scale by suffix (e.g. "md") to lift the box off the page.
- `as` тАФ `ElementType` ┬╖ default `"div"` тАФ Sets which element or tag the box renders as, defaulting to "div" but accepting any semantic HTML element type.
- `p` тАФ `number | string` тАФ Padding on all sides тАФ a spacing-scale step (number) or any CSS length.
- `px` тАФ `number | string` тАФ Horizontal padding (inline start + end) тАФ spacing step or CSS length.
- `py` тАФ `number | string` тАФ Vertical padding (block start + end) тАФ spacing step or CSS length.
- `pt` тАФ `number | string` тАФ Padding top тАФ spacing step or CSS length.
- `pr` тАФ `number | string` тАФ Padding right (inline end) тАФ spacing step or CSS length.
- `pb` тАФ `number | string` тАФ Padding bottom тАФ spacing step or CSS length.
- `pl` тАФ `number | string` тАФ Padding left (inline start) тАФ spacing step or CSS length.
- `m` тАФ `number | string` тАФ Margin on all sides тАФ a spacing-scale step (number) or any CSS length.
- `mx` тАФ `number | string` тАФ Horizontal margin (inline start + end) тАФ spacing step or CSS length.
- `my` тАФ `number | string` тАФ Vertical margin (block start + end) тАФ spacing step or CSS length.
- `mt` тАФ `number | string` тАФ Margin top тАФ spacing step or CSS length.
- `mr` тАФ `number | string` тАФ Margin right (inline end) тАФ spacing step or CSS length.
- `mb` тАФ `number | string` тАФ Margin bottom тАФ spacing step or CSS length.
- `ml` тАФ `number | string` тАФ Margin left (inline start) тАФ spacing step or CSS length.
- `sx` тАФ `Sx` тАФ Style escape hatch: flat CSS goes inline (wins over base); nested selectors/at-rules like "&:hover" or "@media тАж" compile to a scoped stylesheet.

```jsx
import { Box } from "twico-ui";

<Box p={4} bg="surface" border radius="lg" shadow="sm">
  Padded, bordered surface.
</Box>
```

#### Container

Centers content and caps its width with responsive horizontal padding тАФ the outer wrapper for a page or section.

`import { Container } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `size` тАФ `"sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl" | "full" | string` ┬╖ default `"lg"` тАФ Caps the maximum width using a named token (sm through xl, or full) or any CSS length, defaulting to "lg".
- `padded` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Adds responsive horizontal padding so content does not touch the viewport edges; enabled by default, set false to remove it.
- `as` тАФ `ElementType` ┬╖ default `"div"` тАФ Chooses the rendered HTML element or component (such as "section" or "main"), defaulting to "div" for semantic flexibility.
- `sx` тАФ `Sx` тАФ Style escape hatch: flat CSS goes inline (wins over base); nested selectors/at-rules like "&:hover" or "@media тАж" compile to a scoped stylesheet.

```jsx
import { Container, Heading } from "twico-ui";

<Container size="lg">
  <Heading level={1}>Page title</Heading>
</Container>
```

#### Divider

Divider is a thin separator rule that visually splits content, either horizontally or vertically. Use it to break up sections, lists, or inline items, optionally with a centered or aligned label (e.g. "OR").

`import { Divider } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `orientation` тАФ `"horizontal" | "vertical"` ┬╖ default `"horizontal"` тАФ Direction of the separator rule, either horizontal (default) to split stacked content or vertical for inline items.
- `inset` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Indents the start of a horizontal rule to align it with inset content like list item text.
- `align` тАФ `"left" | "center" | "right" | "start" | "end"` ┬╖ default `"center"` тАФ Label alignment when children are provided; logical start/end now accepted and map to left/right (additive тАФ existing left/center/right unchanged).
- `children` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Optional label text rendered within the rule, such as "OR", to break content with a caption.

```jsx
import { Divider } from "twico-ui";

<Divider />

<Divider>OR</Divider>

<span>A</span>
<Divider orientation="vertical" />
<span>B</span>
```

#### Grid

CSS grid primitive тАФ a responsive auto-fill grid (minChildWidth) or a fixed column count (columns).

`import { Grid } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `minChildWidth` тАФ `number | string` тАФ Enables a responsive auto-fill grid where columns wrap once children fall below this minimum width (number is treated as px).
- `columns` тАФ `number` тАФ Sets a fixed number of equal columns, used when minChildWidth is unset for predictable non-responsive layouts.
- `gap` тАФ `number | string` ┬╖ default `4` тАФ Controls spacing between grid cells as a spacing-step number or any CSS length, defaulting to step 4.
- `align` тАФ `string` тАФ Maps to the CSS align-items value, controlling how children are aligned along the block (vertical) axis.
- `justify` тАФ `string` тАФ Maps to the CSS justify-items value, controlling how children are aligned along the inline (horizontal) axis.
- `as` тАФ `ElementType` ┬╖ default `"div"` тАФ Renders the grid as a different element or tag instead of the default div, for semantic markup.
- `sx` тАФ `Sx` тАФ Style escape hatch: flat CSS goes inline (wins over base); nested selectors/at-rules like "&:hover" or "@media тАж" compile to a scoped stylesheet.

```jsx
import { Grid, Card } from "twico-ui";

<Grid minChildWidth={220} gap={4}>
  <Card>One</Card>
  <Card>Two</Card>
  <Card>Three</Card>
</Grid>
```

#### Stack

Flexbox layout primitive тАФ arranges children in a row or column with token-based gaps. Reach for it instead of hand-written flex divs.

`import { Stack } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `direction` тАФ `"row" | "column"` ┬╖ default `"column"` тАФ Sets the main-axis flow, stacking children vertically with "column" (default) or side by side with "row".
- `gap` тАФ `number | string` ┬╖ default `4` тАФ Sets spacing between children, as a numeric spacing step mapped to --space-* or any raw CSS length string.
- `align` тАФ `string` тАФ Maps to the align-items value, controlling how children line up along the cross axis.
- `justify` тАФ `string` тАФ Maps to the justify-content value, controlling how children are distributed along the main axis.
- `wrap` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Allows children to wrap onto multiple lines instead of overflowing when they exceed the container width.
- `inline` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Renders the stack as inline-flex rather than block-level flex, so it sits inline with surrounding content.
- `as` тАФ `ElementType` ┬╖ default `"div"` тАФ Overrides the rendered element or tag (default "div"), useful for semantic wrappers like nav, ul, or section.
- `sx` тАФ `Sx` тАФ Style escape hatch: flat CSS goes inline (wins over base); nested selectors/at-rules like "&:hover" or "@media тАж" compile to a scoped stylesheet.

```jsx
import { Stack } from "twico-ui";

<Stack direction="row" gap={3} align="center" wrap>
  <Button>Save</Button>
  <Button variant="ghost">Cancel</Button>
</Stack>
```

### Typography

#### Code

Inline code with a mono font and a subtle token-styled surface тАФ use it instead of a bare code tag.

`import { Code } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `as` тАФ `ElementType` ┬╖ default `"code"` тАФ Overrides the rendered element, defaulting to a code tag but allowing any tag when the semantics differ.
- `children` тАФ `ReactNode` тАФ Provides the inline code text or nodes shown in the mono font on the subtle token-styled surface.

```jsx
import { Code, Text } from "twico-ui";

<Text>Install with <Code>npm install twico-ui</Code>.</Text>
```

#### Heading

Heading (h1тАУh6) with consistent token typography. Use it instead of bare heading tags.

`import { Heading } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `level` тАФ `1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6` ┬╖ default `2` тАФ Chooses which h1-h6 tag renders and its default token size, keeping document hierarchy and typography consistent.
- `size` тАФ `string` тАФ Overrides the font-size token suffix (e.g. "3xl") to decouple visual scale from the semantic heading level.
- `align` тАФ `string` тАФ Sets the CSS text-align value, letting you left-align, center, or right-align the heading text.
- `as` тАФ `ElementType` тАФ Renders a different tag while preserving the level's size, useful when semantics and visual weight should differ.
- `sx` тАФ `Sx` тАФ Style escape hatch: flat CSS goes inline (wins over base); nested selectors/at-rules like "&:hover" or "@media тАж" compile to a scoped stylesheet.

```jsx
import { Heading } from "twico-ui";

<Heading level={1}>Dashboard</Heading>
<Heading level={3}>Recent activity</Heading>
```

#### Text

Body text with token sizes and semantic color tones. Use it instead of bare paragraph/span tags.

`import { Text } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `size` тАФ `"xs" | "sm" | "base" | "lg" | "xl"` ┬╖ default `"base"` тАФ Picks the font-size token from xs to xl, scaling the body text to fit its context, base by default.
- `tone` тАФ `"default" | "muted" | "subtle" | "primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` ┬╖ default `"default"` тАФ Sets the text color тАФ neutral roles (default, muted, subtle) plus the six semantic color intents.
- `weight` тАФ `string` тАФ Sets the font-weight token suffix like semibold to adjust how heavy the text appears.
- `align` тАФ `string` тАФ Sets the text-align value, controlling whether the text reads left, center, right, or justified.
- `as` тАФ `ElementType` ┬╖ default `"p"` тАФ Chooses the element or tag rendered, defaulting to p so you can swap in span or other tags.
- `sx` тАФ `Sx` тАФ Style escape hatch: flat CSS goes inline (wins over base); nested selectors/at-rules like "&:hover" or "@media тАж" compile to a scoped stylesheet.

```jsx
import { Text } from "twico-ui";

<Text>Default paragraph text.</Text>
<Text size="sm" tone="muted">A muted caption.</Text>
```

### Inputs

#### Checkbox

An accessible checkbox with an animated checkmark, optional label and description, and an indeterminate (mixed) state. Use it for binary on/off choices in forms, or as a "select all" control that can show a partially-selected dash.

`import { Checkbox } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders text or a node beside the box, serving as the clickable caption that describes what the checkbox toggles.
- `description` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Shows secondary helper text below the label to clarify the option's meaning or consequences without crowding it.
- `indeterminate` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Displays the mixed dash state instead of a check, ideal for a select-all that is only partially selected.
- `size` тАФ `"sm" | "md"` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Adjusts the checkbox dimensions between sm and md to match the density of the surrounding form layout.
- `tone` тАФ `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` ┬╖ default `"primary"` тАФ Sets the accent color of the checked/active state from six semantic intents like success, danger, or info.
- `checked` тАФ `boolean` тАФ Drives the checked state in controlled mode, requiring an onChange handler to keep the input in sync.
- `defaultChecked` тАФ `boolean` тАФ Sets the initial checked state for uncontrolled usage, after which the input manages its own value internally.
- `disabled` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Blocks interaction and dims the control when true, signaling the option is currently unavailable to the user.
- `onChange` тАФ `React.ChangeEventHandler<HTMLInputElement>` тАФ Fires on every toggle with the native change event, letting you read the new checked state and update state.
- `id` тАФ `string` тАФ Sets the input's id used to associate the label, auto-generated when omitted so the click target stays connected.
- `className` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `""` тАФ Appends extra class names to the wrapping label element for custom spacing, alignment, or layout overrides.
- `error` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Error message shown below the control; tints the box red and wires aria-invalid/aria-describedby.
- `name` тАФ `string` тАФ Form field name submitted with the value.
- `placeholder` тАФ `string` тАФ Placeholder text shown while the field is empty.
- `readOnly` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Makes the field non-editable while still focusable/selectable.
- `required` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Marks the field required for native form validation.
- `autoComplete` тАФ `string` тАФ Browser autocomplete hint (e.g. "email", "off").

```jsx
import { Checkbox } from "twico-ui";

<Checkbox label="Subscribe to updates" defaultChecked />
<Checkbox label="Select all" indeterminate />
<Checkbox
  label="Notifications"
  description="Get email alerts for new activity"
/>
<Checkbox label="Disabled option" disabled />
<Checkbox label="Compact" size="sm" />
```

#### ColorPicker

ColorPicker is a popover-based color input featuring a saturation/value square, a hue slider, a hex text field, and preset swatches, with the value represented as a hex string. Use it when you need users to choose a color (e.g. a brand or theme color), either controlled via value/onChange or uncontrolled via defaultValue.

`import { ColorPicker } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders an optional caption above the trigger to describe which color the picker controls, such as a brand color.
- `tone` тАФ `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` ┬╖ default `"primary"` тАФ Recolors the hex-input focus accent using one of six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `value` тАФ `string` тАФ Sets the selected hex color in controlled mode, pairing with onChange to keep the displayed color in sync.
- `defaultValue` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"#6366F1"` тАФ Provides the initial hex color for uncontrolled usage, after which the picker tracks selection internally.
- `presets` тАФ `string[]` ┬╖ default `8 default swatches (e.g. #6366F1, #0EA5E9, #14B8A6, ...)` тАФ Lists hex swatch colors shown below the picker for quick one-click selection of common or brand-approved colors.
- `onChange` тАФ `(hex: string) => void` тАФ Fires with the new hex string whenever the user adjusts saturation, hue, the text field, or picks a preset.
- `disabled` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Disable all interaction (the popover cannot be opened).
- `hint` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Helper text shown below when there is no error.
- `error` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Error message тАФ turns the field red and replaces the hint.
- `required` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Marks the field required (adds an asterisk to the label).

```jsx
import { ColorPicker } from "twico-ui";

// Controlled
const [color, setColor] = React.useState("#6366F1");
<ColorPicker label="Brand color" value={color} onChange={setColor} />

// Uncontrolled with custom presets
<ColorPicker
  defaultValue="#14B8A6"
  presets={["#6366F1", "#14B8A6", "#F43F5E"]}
/>
```

#### Combobox

Combobox is a searchable single-select dropdown (MUI-Autocomplete style) where you type directly in the field to filter options, with support for grouped options and two-line (title + subtitle) entries. Use it when a select list is long enough that users benefit from typeahead filtering rather than scrolling.

`import { Combobox } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders a caption above the control to identify the field and improve accessibility for the searchable select.
- `hint` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Shows helper text below the field to guide input, displayed only while no error message is present.
- `error` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Displays an error message, marks the field invalid, and replaces the hint to flag a failed validation.
- `virtualized` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Virtualize the option list тАФ render only the visible slice (plus overscan) so long lists open fast.
- `overscan` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `8` тАФ Extra option rows rendered above/below the viewport when virtualized, smoothing fast scrolling.
- `required` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Adds a required asterisk next to the label, signaling that the user must select a value before submitting.
- `size` тАФ `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Sets the control height across sm, md, or lg to match the density of the surrounding form.
- `tone` тАФ `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` ┬╖ default `"primary"` тАФ Recolors the focus/open accent (border + ring) using one of six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `placeholder` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"SelectтАж"` тАФ Sets the muted text shown in the empty field before the user types to filter or selects an option.
- `options` тАФ `Array<string | ComboboxOption | ComboboxGroup>` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Defines the selectable items as plain strings, value/label/description objects, or grouped option collections.
- `value` тАФ `string | null` тАФ Sets the selected option's value in controlled mode, pairing with onChange to keep the field in sync.
- `defaultValue` тАФ `string | null` ┬╖ default `null` тАФ Provides the initial selection for uncontrolled usage, defaulting to null so the field starts empty.
- `onChange` тАФ `(value: string | null) => void` тАФ Fires with the newly selected value, or null when the selection is cleared, so you can update state.
- `clearable` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Adds a clear button when a value is selected, letting users reset the field back to an empty selection.
- `disabled` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Blocks typing and selection while dimming the field, indicating the control is currently unavailable.
- `placement` тАФ `"bottom" | "top"` ┬╖ default `"bottom"` тАФ Open the menu upward instead of down (e.g. near a viewport bottom).
- `portal` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Render the dropdown in a portal (position:fixed on document.body) so it is never clipped by a scrolling/overflow-hidden ancestor; auto-flips up near the viewport bottom.
- `minWidth` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `0` тАФ Minimum popover width in px when portaled (useful when the control is narrow).
- `name` тАФ `string` тАФ Form field name submitted with the value.
- `readOnly` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Makes the field non-editable while still focusable/selectable.
- `autoComplete` тАФ `string` тАФ Browser autocomplete hint (e.g. "email", "off").

```jsx
import { Combobox } from "twico-ui";

const [country, setCountry] = useState(null);

<Combobox
  label="Country"
  placeholder="Search a country"
  clearable
  value={country}
  onChange={setCountry}
  options={[
    { group: "Asia", options: [
      { value: "id", label: "Indonesia", description: "Jakarta" },
      { value: "jp", label: "Japan", description: "Tokyo" },
    ]},
    { group: "Europe", options: [
      { value: "de", label: "Germany", description: "Berlin" },
    ]},
  ]}
/>
```

#### Currency

Currency is a text input for monetary amounts with a fixed currency defined in code: it renders the currency symbol as a prefix, the currency code as a suffix, and enforces the currency's decimal precision as you type. Use it when the currency is known up front (use CurrencyField when the user should pick one).

`import { Currency } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders a label above the input describing the monetary field, accepting any ReactNode for icons or rich text.
- `hint` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Shows helper text below the input to guide entry, displayed only when no error is present.
- `error` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Displays an error message below the input and marks the field invalid, overriding any hint while set.
- `required` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Marks the field as required and renders an asterisk beside the label; off by default.
- `size` тАФ `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Sets the input height and padding (sm, md, or lg) to match the surrounding form's density, defaulting to "md".
- `tone` тАФ `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` ┬╖ default `"primary"` тАФ Recolors the focus/open accent (border + ring) using one of six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `currency` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"USD"` тАФ Selects the fixed currency by code from the built-in table (e.g. USD, EUR, IDR, JPY), driving symbol and precision.
- `precision` тАФ `number` тАФ Overrides the number of decimal digits enforced while typing, instead of the currency's default precision.
- `symbol` тАФ `string` тАФ Overrides the prefix symbol shown before the amount, useful when the built-in symbol for the currency isn't desired.
- `code` тАФ `string` тАФ Overrides the suffix code displayed after the amount, replacing the default currency code label.
- `value` тАФ `string | number` тАФ Sets the controlled amount as a string or number, keeping the input value driven entirely by your state.
- `defaultValue` тАФ `string | number` ┬╖ default `""` тАФ Sets the initial amount for uncontrolled usage, after which the input manages its own value; defaults to an empty string.
- `onChange` тАФ `(e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => void` тАФ The native change handler receiving the raw input event, useful when you need the underlying DOM event.
- `onValueChange` тАФ `(value: number | null, formatted: string) => void` тАФ Fires with the parsed numeric value (or null) and the formatted display string whenever the amount changes.
- `name` тАФ `string` тАФ Form field name submitted with the value.
- `placeholder` тАФ `string` тАФ Placeholder text shown while the field is empty.
- `disabled` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Disables the control тАФ no interaction and a dimmed appearance.
- `readOnly` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Makes the field non-editable while still focusable/selectable.
- `autoComplete` тАФ `string` тАФ Browser autocomplete hint (e.g. "email", "off").

```jsx
import { Currency } from "twico-ui";

const [price, setPrice] = useState(0);

<Currency label="Price" currency="USD" value={price} onValueChange={setPrice} />
<Currency label="Harga" currency="IDR" />
<Currency label="Total" currency="JPY" />
<Currency label="Custom" symbol="тВ┐" code="BTC" precision={8} />
```

#### CurrencyField

CurrencyField is a currency amount input where the user picks the currency from a searchable dropdown, and that choice drives the prefix symbol, suffix code, and enforced decimal precision. Use it when you need to capture a monetary amount whose currency can be chosen and changed by the user (switching currency re-clamps the amount to the new precision).

`import { CurrencyField } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders a label above the field describing the amount, accepting any ReactNode for icons or formatted text.
- `hint` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Shows helper text below the field to guide entry, displayed only when no error message is present.
- `error` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Displays an error message and marks the field invalid when set, taking visual precedence over the hint.
- `required` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Marks the field as required and renders an asterisk beside the label; off by default.
- `size` тАФ `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Sets the input and dropdown height (sm, md, or lg) to match the surrounding form's density, defaulting to "md".
- `tone` тАФ `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` ┬╖ default `"primary"` тАФ Recolors the focus/open accent (border + ring) using one of six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `currency` тАФ `string` тАФ Controls the selected currency code from your state, keeping the chosen currency driven externally.
- `defaultCurrency` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"USD"` тАФ Sets the initially selected currency code for uncontrolled usage, defaulting to "USD".
- `onCurrencyChange` тАФ `(code: string) => void` тАФ Fires with the new code when the user picks a different currency from the searchable dropdown.
- `currencies` тАФ `Array<string | CurrencyOption>` тАФ Restricts the selectable currencies to the given codes or {value,label} options, instead of the full built-in list.
- `value` тАФ `string | number` тАФ Sets the controlled amount as a string or number, keeping the entered value driven entirely by your state.
- `defaultValue` тАФ `string | number` ┬╖ default `""` тАФ Sets the initial amount for uncontrolled usage, after which the field manages its own value; defaults to an empty string.
- `onValueChange` тАФ `(value: number | null, formatted: string, currency: string) => void` тАФ Fires with the parsed number (or null), the formatted string, and the active currency code whenever either changes.
- `onChange` тАФ `(e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => void` тАФ Native change handler (receives the event); fires alongside onValueChange, matching Currency. Previously typed-out via Omit and silently dropped at runtime.
- `name` тАФ `string` тАФ Form field name submitted with the value.
- `placeholder` тАФ `string` тАФ Placeholder text shown while the field is empty.
- `disabled` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Disables the control тАФ no interaction and a dimmed appearance.
- `readOnly` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Makes the field non-editable while still focusable/selectable.
- `autoComplete` тАФ `string` тАФ Browser autocomplete hint (e.g. "email", "off").

```jsx
import { CurrencyField } from "twico-ui";

<CurrencyField
  label="Amount"
  defaultCurrency="USD"
  onValueChange={(n, str, code) => {}}
/>

{/* limit the currency choices */}
<CurrencyField
  label="Amount"
  defaultCurrency="EUR"
  currencies={["USD", "EUR", "GBP", "IDR"]}
/>
```

#### DatePicker

DatePicker is a calendar-popover date input with month/year navigation, a month grid, today highlight, min/max bounds, and a clearable value. Use it when you need users to select a single date with optional range limits and custom formatting.

`import { DatePicker } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Optional field label rendered above the input to name the control and associate it for accessibility.
- `tone` тАФ `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` ┬╖ default `"primary"` тАФ Recolors the focus/open accent (border + ring) using one of six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `value` тАФ `Date | null` тАФ Controlled selected date, or null when empty; pair it with onChange to own the value in state.
- `defaultValue` тАФ `Date | null` ┬╖ default `null` тАФ Initial date for uncontrolled usage when value is omitted, defaulting to null so the field starts empty.
- `placeholder` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"Select date"` тАФ Text shown in the trigger when no date is selected, defaulting to "Select date" to prompt the user.
- `min` тАФ `Date` тАФ Earliest selectable date; dates before it are disabled in the calendar grid to enforce a lower bound.
- `max` тАФ `Date` тАФ Latest selectable date; dates after it are disabled in the calendar grid to enforce an upper bound.
- `disabled` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Disables the control and prevents the calendar popover from opening, useful for read-only or gated form states.
- `clearable` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Shows a clear (├Ч) button when a date is set so users can reset the field, enabled by default.
- `format` тАФ `(date: Date) => string` тАФ Custom formatter that turns the selected Date into display text, defaulting to a localized medium date string.
- `editable` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Renders the trigger as a typeable text input with a calendar toggle; typed text commits via parse on Enter/blur.
- `parse` тАФ `(text: string) => Date | null` тАФ Parses typed text into a Date when editable; return null or an invalid Date to reject, defaults to a lenient Date.parse.
- `weekStartsOn` тАФ `0 | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6` ┬╖ default `0` тАФ Widened from 0|1 to any weekday; controls which day the calendar grid + weekday header start on (0 = Sunday).
- `onChange` тАФ `(date: Date | null) => void` тАФ Fires with the newly selected Date, or null when the value is cleared, so you can update state.
- `hint` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Helper text shown below when there is no error.
- `error` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Error message тАФ turns the field red and replaces the hint.
- `required` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Marks the field required (adds an asterisk to the label).
- `locale` тАФ `string` тАФ BCP-47 locale for Intl-derived month/weekday names and date formatting; omit for the runtime default.

```jsx
import { DatePicker } from "twico-ui";

const [date, setDate] = useState(null);

<DatePicker
  label="Start date"
  value={date}
  onChange={setDate}
  min={new Date()}
  weekStartsOn={1}
/>
```

#### DateRangePicker

DateRangePicker is a calendar input for selecting a start and end date with click-start/click-end selection, in-range highlighting, hover preview, and quick presets (Last 7/14/30/90 days). Use it when a form needs a reporting period or any bounded date span.

`import { DateRangePicker } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Optional field label rendered above the control to name the date range input and aid accessibility.
- `tone` тАФ `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` ┬╖ default `"primary"` тАФ Recolors the focus/open accent (border + ring) using one of six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `value` тАФ `DateRange` тАФ Controlled range as a { start, end } object; pair it with onChange to own the selection in state.
- `defaultValue` тАФ `DateRange` ┬╖ default `{ start: null, end: null }` тАФ Initial range for uncontrolled usage, defaulting to { start: null, end: null } so the field starts empty.
- `placeholder` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"Select date range"` тАФ Text shown in the trigger when no range is selected, defaulting to "Select date range" to prompt the user.
- `presets` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Shows the quick-preset column (Last 7/14/30/90 days) for fast range selection, enabled by default.
- `weekStartsOn` тАФ `0 | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6` ┬╖ default `0` тАФ Widened from 0|1 to any weekday; controls which day the calendar grid + weekday header start on (0 = Sunday).
- `editable` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Renders the trigger as a typeable input accepting "start тАУ end"; each side commits via parse on Enter/blur.
- `parse` тАФ `(text: string) => Date | null` тАФ Parses one side of the typed range into a Date when editable; return null or invalid to reject, defaults to Date.parse.
- `onChange` тАФ `(range: DateRange) => void` тАФ Fires with the updated { start, end } range whenever the selection changes so you can update state.
- `disabled` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Disable all interaction (the popover cannot be opened).
- `hint` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Helper text shown below when there is no error.
- `error` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Error message тАФ turns the field red and replaces the hint.
- `required` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Marks the field required (adds an asterisk to the label).
- `locale` тАФ `string` тАФ BCP-47 locale for Intl-derived month/weekday names and date formatting; omit for the runtime default.

```jsx
import { DateRangePicker } from "twico-ui";

const [range, setRange] = useState({ start: null, end: null });

<DateRangePicker
  label="Reporting period"
  value={range}
  onChange={setRange}
  weekStartsOn={1}
/>
```

#### Field

Field is the reusable form-field wrapper that supplies the label, required asterisk, helper hint, and error message (with the matching ARIA ids) that Input and Select already use internally. Wrap any control with it to get consistent field spacing, the danger-colored error treatment, and the aria-describedby target without re-implementing the markup.

`import { Field } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Field label rendered above the control.
- `hint` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Helper text shown below when there is no error.
- `error` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Error message тАФ replaces the hint and renders in the danger color.
- `required` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Marks the field required, adding an asterisk after the label.
- `htmlFor` тАФ `string` тАФ Id of the control the <label> binds to (sets the label's htmlFor).
- `size` тАФ `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Spacing scale for parity with Input/Select; exposed via data-size on the root.
- `id` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `auto` тАФ Base id; the hint/error element gets `${id}-desc` for your control's aria-describedby.
- `children` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ The control(s) the field wraps.

```jsx
import { Field } from "twico-ui";

// Wrap any control for the shared label/hint/error chrome. Give Field an id and set
// the control's aria-describedby to that id followed by -desc to announce hint/error.
<Field label="Email" id="email" hint="We'll never share it." required htmlFor="email">
  <input id="email" type="email" aria-describedby="email-desc" />
</Field>

<Field label="Token" id="tok" error="That token is invalid." htmlFor="tok">
  <input id="tok" aria-invalid aria-describedby="tok-desc" />
</Field>
```

#### FileUpload

FileUpload is a drag-and-drop dropzone with a click-to-browse fallback and a removable list of selected files. Use it when you need users to attach one or more files, either controlled via value/onChange or uncontrolled.

`import { FileUpload } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `accept` тАФ `string` тАФ Restricts selectable files using a native input accept string such as "image/*,.pdf", filtering the browse dialog and drops.
- `tone` тАФ `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` ┬╖ default `"primary"` тАФ Recolors the dropzone focus ring and drag-active border using one of six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `multiple` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Allows selecting and attaching more than one file at a time when true; defaults to single-file selection.
- `disabled` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Disables the dropzone and underlying input when true, blocking clicks, drops, and any further file changes.
- `hint` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders a hint line under the prompt, defaulting to the accepted types or "Any file" when not provided.
- `value` тАФ `File[]` тАФ Supplies the controlled array of selected File objects, making the component fully managed by parent state.
- `onChange` тАФ `(files: File[]) => void` тАФ Fires with the updated File array whenever files are added or removed, letting you sync controlled state.
- `defaultValue` тАФ `File[]` ┬╖ default `[]` тАФ Uncontrolled initial file list.
- `label` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Field label rendered above the dropzone.
- `error` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Error message shown below the dropzone; turns the border red (sets aria-invalid).
- `required` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Marks the field required (adds an asterisk to the label).

```jsx
import { FileUpload } from "twico-ui";

const [files, setFiles] = React.useState([]);

<FileUpload
  multiple
  accept="image/*,.pdf"
  value={files}
  onChange={setFiles}
  hint="PNG, JPG or PDF up to 10MB"
/>
```

#### Input

A text input with optional label, hint text, error/validation state, and inline leading/trailing icons. Use it for any single-line form field; with type="password" it gets a built-in reveal/hide eye toggle.

`import { Input } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders a label node above the control so the field has an accessible, visible name for the user.
- `hint` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Shows helper text below the input to guide entry; it is hidden once an error message is present.
- `error` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Displays a validation error below the field, switches the control to its red invalid state, and replaces the hint.
- `required` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Marks the field as required and appends an asterisk to the label to signal mandatory input; defaults to false.
- `size` тАФ `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Sets the control height and padding (sm, md, or lg) to match the density of the surrounding form.
- `tone` тАФ `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` ┬╖ default `"primary"` тАФ Recolors the focus/open accent (border + ring) using one of six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `leftIcon` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders an icon node inside the field on the leading edge, useful as a prefix like a search or mail glyph.
- `rightIcon` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders a trailing icon node inside the field, overriding the built-in password reveal toggle when supplied.
- `leftAddon` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Bordered text addon flush against the leading edge (e.g. https://, @), distinct from the inline leftIcon.
- `rightAddon` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Bordered text addon flush against the trailing edge (e.g. .00, kg, %).
- `showCount` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ With maxLength, renders a live current / max character counter on the hint/error line, danger-toned near the limit.
- `onChange` тАФ `(e: React.ChangeEvent) => void` тАФ Native change handler on the underlying form control.
- `name` тАФ `string` тАФ Form field name submitted with the value.
- `placeholder` тАФ `string` тАФ Placeholder text shown while the field is empty.
- `disabled` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Disables the control тАФ no interaction and a dimmed appearance.
- `readOnly` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Makes the field non-editable while still focusable/selectable.
- `autoComplete` тАФ `string` тАФ Browser autocomplete hint (e.g. "email", "off").

```jsx
import { Input } from "twico-ui";

const SearchIcon = () => (<svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="2" strokeLinecap="round"><circle cx="11" cy="11" r="8" /><path d="m21 21-4.3-4.3" /></svg>);

<Input label="Email" type="email" placeholder="you@twico.dev" required />
<Input label="Search" leftIcon={<SearchIcon />} placeholder="SearchтАж" />
<Input label="Amount" rightIcon={<span>USD</span>} placeholder="0.00" />
<Input label="Password" type="password" placeholder="тАвтАвтАвтАвтАвтАвтАвтАв" />
<Input label="Username" hint="Choose a unique handle" placeholder="twico" />
<Input label="Email" type="email" error="Invalid email address" defaultValue="nope" />
```

#### MultiSelect

MultiSelect is a MUI-Autocomplete-style multi-select where chosen values appear as removable chips and you type inline to filter a checkable options overlay. Use it when users need to pick several values from a list that benefits from search, grouped headings, or two-line (label + description) options.

`import { MultiSelect } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `options` тАФ `Array<string | MultiSelectOption | MultiSelectGroup>` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Supplies the selectable options as plain strings, {value,label,description} objects, or {group,options} grouped sections.
- `label` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders a label node above the control so the multi-select has an accessible, visible name for the user.
- `hint` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Shows helper text below the control to guide selection; it is hidden once an error message is present.
- `error` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Displays a validation error below the control and styles it as invalid to flag a selection problem.
- `virtualized` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Virtualize the option list тАФ render only the visible slice (plus overscan) so long lists open fast.
- `overscan` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `8` тАФ Extra option rows rendered above/below the viewport when virtualized, smoothing fast scrolling.
- `required` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Marks the field as required and adds an asterisk to the label to signal a mandatory selection; defaults to false.
- `placeholder` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"SelectтАж"` тАФ Sets the prompt text shown when no chips are selected, defaulting to "SelectтАж" to invite a choice.
- `value` тАФ `string[]` тАФ Controls the selected values as a string array, making the component fully controlled when paired with onChange.
- `defaultValue` тАФ `string[]` ┬╖ default `[]` тАФ Seeds the initially selected values for uncontrolled usage, defaulting to an empty array of no selections.
- `onChange` тАФ `(values: string[]) => void` тАФ Fires with the new array of selected values whenever a chip is added or removed from the selection.
- `disabled` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Disables the control and blocks all interaction when set to true, greying out the field; defaults to false.
- `size` тАФ `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Control height preset, matching Select/Combobox.
- `tone` тАФ `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` ┬╖ default `"primary"` тАФ Recolors the focus/open accent (border + ring) using one of six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `clearable` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Show a clear-all (├Ч) affix when at least one value is selected.
- `placement` тАФ `"bottom" | "top"` ┬╖ default `"bottom"` тАФ Open the menu upward instead of down (e.g. near a viewport bottom).
- `portal` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Render the dropdown in a portal (position:fixed on document.body) so it is never clipped by a scrolling/overflow-hidden ancestor; auto-flips up near the viewport bottom.
- `minWidth` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `0` тАФ Minimum popover width in px when portaled (useful when the control is narrow).
- `name` тАФ `string` тАФ Form field name submitted with the value.
- `readOnly` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Makes the field non-editable while still focusable/selectable.
- `autoComplete` тАФ `string` тАФ Browser autocomplete hint (e.g. "email", "off").

```jsx
import { MultiSelect } from "twico-ui";

const [tags, setTags] = useState(["react", "tailwind"]);

<MultiSelect
  label="Tech stack"
  placeholder="Add technologies"
  value={tags}
  onChange={setTags}
  options={[
    { group: "Frameworks", options: [
      { value: "react", label: "React", description: "UI library" },
      { value: "vue", label: "Vue", description: "Progressive framework" },
    ]},
    { group: "Tooling", options: ["tailwind", "typescript"] },
  ]}
/>
```

#### Radio

Radio is a single radio button rendered with an optional label and description. Use several Radio inputs sharing the same `name` to let users pick exactly one option from a group.

`import { Radio } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Text or node rendered beside the radio dot, naming the option the user selects when choosing it.
- `description` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Secondary helper text shown under the label to add context or clarify what selecting the option means.
- `size` тАФ `"sm" | "md"` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Visual size of the radio control, sm or md, to align with the density of nearby form fields.
- `tone` тАФ `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` ┬╖ default `"primary"` тАФ Sets the accent color of the checked/active state from six semantic intents like success, danger, or info.
- `error` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Error message shown below the control; tints the dot red and wires aria-invalid/aria-describedby.
- `onChange` тАФ `(e: React.ChangeEvent) => void` тАФ Native change handler on the underlying form control.
- `name` тАФ `string` тАФ Form field name submitted with the value.
- `placeholder` тАФ `string` тАФ Placeholder text shown while the field is empty.
- `disabled` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Disables the control тАФ no interaction and a dimmed appearance.
- `readOnly` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Makes the field non-editable while still focusable/selectable.
- `required` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Marks the field required for native form validation.
- `autoComplete` тАФ `string` тАФ Browser autocomplete hint (e.g. "email", "off").

```jsx
import { Radio } from "twico-ui";

<Radio name="plan" value="free" label="Free" defaultChecked />
<Radio
  name="plan"
  value="pro"
  label="Pro"
  description="$12 / month"
/>
```

#### Rating

Rating is a star-rating input that works either as an interactive control (click or hover to set a score) or as a read-only display. Use it to capture user feedback like reviews or to show an existing score; clicking the current star again clears the rating to 0.

`import { Rating } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `value` тАФ `number` тАФ Controlled rating from 0 to count, letting the parent own the score and re-render when it changes.
- `defaultValue` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `0` тАФ Initial score for uncontrolled usage, defaulting to 0 so no stars start filled until the user interacts.
- `count` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `5` тАФ Total number of stars rendered, defaulting to 5, which sets the maximum selectable score.
- `size` тАФ `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Sets the star size (sm, md, or lg) to match the surrounding text density, defaulting to md.
- `tone` тАФ `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` ┬╖ default `"warning"` тАФ Sets the filled-star color from six semantic intents; defaults to warning (gold). The explicit color prop overrides it.
- `color` тАФ `string` тАФ Overrides the filled-star color with any CSS color, falling back to the warning/amber token when omitted.
- `readOnly` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Renders the stars as a non-interactive display only, useful for showing an existing score without accepting input.
- `showValue` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Displays the numeric score beside the stars (clean integer, or one decimal for fractional values).
- `clearable` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Allow clearing back to 0 тАФ clicking the selected star, or pressing Delete/Backspace, resets to no rating.
- `format` тАФ `(value: number) => React.ReactNode` тАФ Custom formatter for the showValue badge and the read-only accessible label (e.g. custom precision, i18n, a /5 suffix).
- `name` тАФ `string` тАФ Name for a hidden form field so the rating value participates in native form submission.
- `onChange` тАФ `(value: number) => void` тАФ Fires with the new rating when the user clicks a star, or 0 when they clear the current selection.
- `disabled` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Disable all interaction and dim the control.

```jsx
import { Rating } from "twico-ui";

// Interactive
const [r, setR] = React.useState(0);
<Rating value={r} onChange={setR} />

// Read-only display with numeric value
<Rating value={4} readOnly showValue />

// Uncontrolled, larger stars
<Rating defaultValue={3} size="lg" count={5} />
```

#### Select

A custom select that replaces the native browser dropdown with a rounded, keyboard-navigable popover, supporting grouped options and two-line (title + subtitle) options. Use it when you need a styled single-select with optional in-popover search, grouping, or descriptions.

`import { Select } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders a field label above the trigger to identify what the select controls within a form.
- `hint` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Shows helper text below the trigger to guide selection, automatically hidden once an error message is set.
- `error` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Displays an error message and marks the field invalid, replacing the hint to flag failed validation.
- `virtualized` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Virtualize the option list тАФ render only the visible slice (plus overscan) so long lists open fast.
- `overscan` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `8` тАФ Extra option rows rendered above/below the viewport when virtualized, smoothing fast scrolling.
- `required` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Appends a required asterisk to the label, signaling that a selection must be made before submitting.
- `size` тАФ `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Controls the trigger height (sm, md, or lg) to match the density of nearby form controls, defaulting to md.
- `tone` тАФ `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` ┬╖ default `"primary"` тАФ Recolors the focus/open accent (border + ring) using one of six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `placeholder` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"SelectтАж"` тАФ Text shown in the trigger when no value is selected, defaulting to "SelectтАж" as a prompt to choose.
- `searchPlaceholder` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"SearchтАж"` тАФ Placeholder text for the in-popover search box, defaulting to "SearchтАж" to invite filtering of options.
- `searchable` тАФ `boolean` тАФ Shows the filter search box inside the popover, auto-enabling when more than five options exist if left unset.
- `options` тАФ `Array<string | SelectOption | SelectGroup>` ┬╖ **required** тАФ The selectable items as plain strings, {value,label,description} objects, or {group,options} groups for two-line or grouped lists.
- `value` тАФ `string | null` тАФ Controlled selected value as a string or null, letting the parent own which option is currently chosen.
- `defaultValue` тАФ `string | null` ┬╖ default `null` тАФ Initial selected value for uncontrolled usage, defaulting to null so nothing is selected at first.
- `onChange` тАФ `(value: string) => void` тАФ Fires with the chosen value string when the user selects an option from the popover.
- `disabled` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Disables the trigger so the popover cannot open and no selection can be made.
- `placement` тАФ `"bottom" | "top"` ┬╖ default `"bottom"` тАФ Opens the menu downward or upward from the trigger, defaulting to "bottom" for the typical dropdown direction.
- `portal` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Renders the dropdown in a body portal so it is never clipped, auto-flipping when near the viewport bottom.
- `minWidth` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `0` тАФ Sets the minimum popover width in pixels when portaled, ensuring the menu stays at least this wide.
- `clearable` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Show a clear (├Ч) affix when a value is selected; Delete/Backspace on the closed trigger also clears. Emits onChange(null).

```jsx
import { Select } from "twico-ui";

const [v, setV] = useState(null);

<Select
  label="Assignee"
  placeholder="Pick a teammate"
  value={v}
  onChange={setV}
  options={[
    { group: "Design", options: [
      { value: "ada", label: "Ada Park", description: "Product designer" },
      { value: "sam", label: "Sam Lee", description: "Brand designer" },
    ]},
    { group: "Engineering", options: [
      { value: "jo", label: "Jo Kim", description: "Frontend" },
    ]},
  ]}
/>
```

#### Slider

Slider is a range input with a filled track, draggable thumb, a value bubble that appears while dragging, optional step ticks, and full keyboard support (arrows, Home, End). Use it when users need to pick a number within a range, such as volume, price, or other continuous settings.

`import { Slider } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders a label above the track to describe what the slider adjusts, such as volume or price.
- `tone` тАФ `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` ┬╖ default `"primary"` тАФ Sets the filled-track and thumb accent color from six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `value` тАФ `number` тАФ Controlled current value, letting the parent own the slider position and re-render as the number changes.
- `defaultValue` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `0` тАФ Initial value for uncontrolled usage, defaulting to 0 so the thumb starts at the low end.
- `min` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `0` тАФ Lowest value the slider can reach, defaulting to 0, defining the start of the selectable range.
- `max` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `100` тАФ Highest value the slider can reach, defaulting to 100, defining the end of the selectable range.
- `step` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `1` тАФ Increment between selectable values, defaulting to 1, which controls how finely the thumb can be positioned.
- `disabled` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Disables dragging and keyboard input while dimming the slider to show it is currently inactive.
- `showValue` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Displays the current value at the top-right above the track when true (the default), for an always-visible readout.
- `showTicks` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Renders step ticks along the rail when enabled, helping users see and snap to discrete increments.
- `formatValue` тАФ `(value: number) => React.ReactNode` тАФ Formats the displayed value in the label and drag bubble, useful for adding units, currency, or custom text.
- `range` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Enable dual-thumb range mode (also auto-enabled by a [number, number] value/defaultValue); onChange then emits a [min, max] tuple.
- `pageStep` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `Math.max(step, (max-min)/10)` тАФ Large-step increment for PageUp/PageDown keys; defaults to ~10% of the range.
- `precision` тАФ `number` тАФ Decimal places for the default display and aria value; derived from step when unset (so a 0.1 step shows one decimal).
- `getAriaValueText` тАФ `(value: number) => string` тАФ Human-readable value for aria-valuetext; use when formatValue returns a node so assistive tech still announces a meaningful value.
- `name` тАФ `string` тАФ Name for a hidden form field so the slider value participates in native form submission.
- `onChange` тАФ `(value: number) => void` тАФ Fires with the new number whenever the value changes via drag or keyboard, keeping state in sync.
- `hint` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Helper text shown below the track when there is no error.
- `error` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Error message shown below the track; turns it red (sets aria-invalid) and replaces the hint.

```jsx
import { Slider } from "twico-ui";

const [vol, setVol] = React.useState(40);

<Slider label="Volume" value={vol} onChange={setVol} />

<Slider
  label="Price"
  min={0}
  max={1000}
  step={50}
  showTicks
  defaultValue={250}
  formatValue={(v) => `$${v}`}
/>
```

#### Switch

Switch is a toggle control with a springy thumb for instant on/off settings, optionally labeled with a title and supporting description. Use it for binary settings that apply immediately, like enabling dark mode or notifications.

`import { Switch } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders the title text shown beside the switch, naming the setting it toggles like "Enable notifications".
- `description` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders secondary helper text below the label to explain what the setting does or its consequences.
- `size` тАФ `"sm" | "md"` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Sets the visual size of the track and thumb (sm or md) to match the density of the surrounding form.
- `tone` тАФ `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` ┬╖ default `"primary"` тАФ Sets the ON-track accent color from six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `error` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Error message shown below the control; tints the track red and wires aria-invalid/aria-describedby.
- `onChange` тАФ `(e: React.ChangeEvent) => void` тАФ Native change handler on the underlying form control.
- `name` тАФ `string` тАФ Form field name submitted with the value.
- `placeholder` тАФ `string` тАФ Placeholder text shown while the field is empty.
- `disabled` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Disables the control тАФ no interaction and a dimmed appearance.
- `readOnly` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Makes the field non-editable while still focusable/selectable.
- `required` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Marks the field required for native form validation.
- `autoComplete` тАФ `string` тАФ Browser autocomplete hint (e.g. "email", "off").

```jsx
import { Switch } from "twico-ui";

<Switch label="Dark mode" defaultChecked />

<Switch
  label="Email notifications"
  description="Send me product updates"
/>

<Switch label="Compact view" size="sm" />
```

#### Textarea

Textarea is a multi-line text input with an optional label, hint text, and error state for longer-form content. Use it when collecting paragraph-length input like bios, descriptions, or comments.

`import { Textarea } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders a field label above the textarea, identifying what longer-form input the control collects.
- `hint` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Shows helper text below the textarea to guide input, hidden whenever an error message is present.
- `error` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Displays an error message and marks the field invalid when set, replacing the hint text below.
- `required` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Adds a required asterisk beside the label when true, signaling that the field must be filled in.
- `size` тАФ `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Control size тАФ same padding steps as Input.
- `tone` тАФ `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` ┬╖ default `"primary"` тАФ Recolors the focus/open accent (border + ring) using one of six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `autosize` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Grows the textarea to fit its content between minRows and maxRows, hiding the manual resize handle.
- `minRows` тАФ `number` тАФ Minimum rows when autosize is on (falls back to rows).
- `maxRows` тАФ `number` тАФ Maximum rows when autosize is on; taller content scrolls internally.
- `showCount` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ With maxLength, renders a live current / max character counter, danger-toned near the limit.
- `onChange` тАФ `(e: React.ChangeEvent) => void` тАФ Native change handler on the underlying form control.
- `name` тАФ `string` тАФ Form field name submitted with the value.
- `placeholder` тАФ `string` тАФ Placeholder text shown while the field is empty.
- `rows` тАФ `number` тАФ Visible number of text rows.
- `disabled` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Disables the control тАФ no interaction and a dimmed appearance.
- `readOnly` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Makes the field non-editable while still focusable/selectable.

```jsx
import { Textarea } from "twico-ui";

<Textarea
  label="Bio"
  rows={4}
  placeholder="Tell us about yourself"
  hint="Max 280 characters"
/>
```

### Data display

#### Avatar

Avatar displays a user's image with a graceful fallback to initials and an optional presence dot. Use it to represent people or entities in lists, headers, comments, and profile menus.

`import { Avatar } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `src` тАФ `string` тАФ The image URL to display, gracefully falling back to derived initials when it is missing or fails to load.
- `name` тАФ `string` тАФ The person's full name, used to derive the displayed initials and the avatar's accessible label.
- `size` тАФ `"xs" | "sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl"` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Sets the overall avatar dimensions from xs to xl to suit lists, headers, comments, or profile menus.
- `square` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ When true, renders the avatar as a rounded square instead of the default circle for a different visual style.
- `status` тАФ `"online" | "busy" | "away" | "offline"` тАФ Shows a small presence dot indicating online, busy, away, or offline state on the avatar.
- `ring` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ When true, adds a brand-colored ring around the avatar to emphasize or highlight the user.

```jsx
import { Avatar } from "twico-ui";

<Avatar src="/u/jane.jpg" name="Jane Doe" />
<Avatar name="Sam Lee" status="online" />
<Avatar name="Twico" size="lg" square ring />
```

#### AvatarMenu

AvatarMenu is a user avatar that opens a portaled account dropdown with a rich header (avatar, name, email) above the menu items. Use it for the classic account menu тАФ avatar-only in a navbar, or with name/email/chevron in a sidebar.

`import { AvatarMenu } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `name` тАФ `string` тАФ The user's full name, driving the derived initials and the rich header shown atop the dropdown.
- `src` тАФ `string` тАФ The avatar image URL shown in both the trigger and the dropdown header, falling back to initials when absent.
- `email` тАФ `string` тАФ The email address shown in the dropdown header, and in the trigger when showName is enabled.
- `subtitle` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Overrides the secondary line in the header and trigger, defaulting to the email when not provided.
- `status` тАФ `"online" | "busy" | "away" | "offline"` тАФ Shows a presence dot indicating online, busy, away, or offline state on the trigger avatar.
- `size` тАФ `"xs" | "sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl"` ┬╖ default `"sm"` тАФ Sets the trigger avatar dimensions from xs to xl to fit a compact navbar or a roomier sidebar.
- `items` тАФ `MenuItemDef[]` ┬╖ **required** тАФ The account menu entries to render in the dropdown, using the same MenuItemDef shape as Menu's items.
- `showName` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ When true, displays the name and email beside the avatar in the trigger instead of showing the avatar alone.
- `showChevron` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `showName` тАФ When true, shows a chevron in the trigger to hint at the dropdown, defaulting to the showName value.
- `align` тАФ `"start" | "end"` ┬╖ default `"end"` тАФ Aligns the dropdown's start or end edge to the trigger, defaulting to end for right-anchored account menus.

```jsx
import { AvatarMenu } from "twico-ui";

<AvatarMenu
  name="Ada Park"
  email="ada@twico.dev"
  status="online"
  showName
  items={[
    { label: "Profile", onClick: () => {} },
    { label: "Settings", shortcut: "тМШ,", onClick: () => {} },
    { separator: true },
    { label: "Sign out", danger: true, onClick: () => {} },
  ]}
/>
```

#### Badge

Badge is a compact status label or count chip available in six semantic tones and three fill styles. Use it to flag state (active, pending, draft), categories, or small counts inline next to text, list items, or table rows.

`import { Badge } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
