- `tone` тАФ `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` ┬╖ default `"primary"` тАФ Sets the semantic color from six options like success or danger to convey the badge's state or category.
- `variant` тАФ `"soft" | "solid" | "outline"` ┬╖ default `"soft"` тАФ Chooses the fill style between soft, solid, and outline to control how prominently the badge reads.
- `size` тАФ `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Adds a new "sm" option (smaller font padding/height than md) to the existing size scale; md/lg unchanged and md remains the default.
- `dot` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ When true, shows a small leading status dot before the label to reinforce the badge's state.
- `children` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ The badge content, typically a short status label or a small count shown inline next to text.

```jsx
import { Badge } from "twico-ui";

<Badge tone="success" dot>Active</Badge>
<Badge tone="warning" variant="solid">Pending</Badge>
<Badge tone="neutral" variant="outline">Draft</Badge>
<Badge tone="info" size="lg">12 new</Badge>
```

#### Boxplot

A dependency-free inline-SVG box-and-whisker chart that draws one box per category from a five-number summary (min, Q1, median, Q3, max) with whiskers and optional outlier points. Reach for it to compare the spread, skew, and outliers of distributions across groups where a bar or line chart would hide the shape of the data.

`import { Boxplot } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `data` тАФ `BoxplotDatum[]` ┬╖ **required** тАФ The boxes to plot, one per category, each a five-number summary with an optional array of outlier values.
- `color` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"var(--color-primary)"` тАФ Box fill and stroke accent, accepting any CSS color including a design token reference.
- `height` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `300` тАФ Pixel height of the chart; the width fills its container at 100%.
- `showGrid` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Whether to draw horizontal grid lines at the value axis ticks behind the boxes.
- `showAxis` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Whether to render the value axis and the category labels under each box.
- `valueFormat` тАФ `(value: number) => string` ┬╖ default `toLocaleString` тАФ Formatter applied to values in the per-box tooltip and the hidden data table.
- `ariaLabel` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"box plot"` тАФ Accessible name for the chart's role="img" SVG; per-box values are exposed via the hidden data table.
- `tableFallback` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Renders a visually-hidden data table as an accessible text alternative for screen readers (WCAG 1.1.1).
- `caption` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Caption for the hidden data table; defaults to the chart's accessible label when omitted.

```jsx
import { Boxplot } from "twico-ui";

<Boxplot
  data={[
    { label: "API", min: 12, q1: 24, median: 33, q3: 42, max: 58, outliers: [72] },
    { label: "Web", min: 18, q1: 30, median: 41, q3: 52, max: 66 },
    { label: "Cache", min: 8, q1: 20, median: 27, q3: 38, max: 49 },
  ]}
  valueFormat={(v) => `${v}ms`}
/>
```

#### BubbleChart

A dependency-free SVG bubble chart тАФ a ScatterChart preset that sizes each dot by a third value (z) across two nice-scaled numeric axes. Reach for it when you need to show a third dimension (volume, revenue, weight) on top of an x/y relationship.

`import { BubbleChart } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `series` тАФ `ScatterSeries[]` ┬╖ **required** тАФ The series to plot; each has an optional name/color and a list of { x, y, z?, label? } points.
- `bubble` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Size the dots by their z value; on by default for BubbleChart, and also auto-enabled when any point has a numeric z.
- `maxBubble` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `26` тАФ Maximum bubble radius in pixels тАФ the largest z value maps to this radius, with area scaling as radius тИЭ тИЪz.
- `dotRadius` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `4.5` тАФ Fixed dot radius in pixels used when bubble sizing is turned off.
- `height` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `300` тАФ Pixel height of the chart, which also sets the SVG aspect ratio so dots stay round.
- `xLabel` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Axis title drawn beneath the x axis.
- `yLabel` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Axis title drawn, rotated, beside the y axis.
- `showGrid` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Whether to render the background grid lines behind the plotted points.
- `showLegend` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true when >1 series` тАФ Whether to show the series legend; defaults on when there is more than one series.
- `xFormat` тАФ `(value: number) => string` тАФ Formatter for x values in tooltips and the hidden table; defaults to valueFormat.
- `yFormat` тАФ `(value: number) => string` тАФ Formatter for y values in tooltips and the hidden table; defaults to valueFormat.
- `valueFormat` тАФ `(value: number) => string` тАФ Fallback value formatter for x/y/z, using locale-grouped numbers by default.
- `ariaLabel` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"bubble chart"` тАФ Accessible name for the chart's role="img" SVG; per-point values are exposed via the hidden data table instead.
- `tableFallback` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Render a visually-hidden data table as an accessible text alternative to the SVG (WCAG 1.1.1).
- `caption` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Caption for the hidden data table; defaults to the chart's accessible label.

```jsx
import { BubbleChart } from "twico-ui";

<BubbleChart
  series={[
    { name: "Plans", points: [
      { x: 12, y: 40, z: 800, label: "Starter" },
      { x: 26, y: 65, z: 2400, label: "Team" },
      { x: 48, y: 82, z: 5200, label: "Business" },
    ] },
  ]}
  xLabel="Seats"
  yLabel="Satisfaction"
/>
```

#### Candlestick

An OHLC candlestick chart that draws one candle per period тАФ a highтЖТlow wick and an openтЖТclose body colored by direction тАФ as dependency-free inline SVG. Reach for it to visualize price or range data over time; use Chart for bars, lines and areas.

`import { Candlestick } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `data` тАФ `CandlestickDatum[]` ┬╖ **required** тАФ OHLC data points, one candle each ({ label, open, high, low, close }), given in chronological order.
- `upColor` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"var(--color-success)"` тАФ Color for up candles where close is greater than or equal to open, accepting any CSS color.
- `downColor` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"var(--color-danger)"` тАФ Color for down candles where close is less than open, accepting any CSS color.
- `height` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `300` тАФ Pixel height of the chart; the width always fills the container at 100 percent.
- `showGrid` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Whether to draw the horizontal value grid lines behind the candles.
- `showAxis` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Whether to render the value axis and the category (x) labels, which thin out automatically when dense.
- `valueFormat` тАФ `(value: number) => string` ┬╖ default `toLocaleString` тАФ Formats values in the per-candle tooltip and the hidden data-table cells.
- `ariaLabel` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"candlestick chart"` тАФ Accessible name for the chart's SVG role="img"; aria-label is also accepted as an alternative.
- `tableFallback` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Renders a visually-hidden data table as an accessible text alternative to the SVG (WCAG 1.1.1).
- `caption` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Caption for the hidden data table; defaults to the chart's accessible label when omitted.

```jsx
import { Candlestick } from "twico-ui";

<Candlestick
  valueFormat={(v) => `$${v}`}
  data={[
    { label: "Mon", open: 132, high: 138, low: 130, close: 136 },
    { label: "Tue", open: 136, high: 141, low: 134, close: 135 },
    { label: "Wed", open: 135, high: 137, low: 128, close: 129 },
  ]}
/>
```

#### Card

Card is a surface container for grouping related content, with an optional header (title/subtitle), body, and footer. Use it to present grouped information such as stats, summaries, or actions, optionally making it interactive with a hover-lift effect.

`import { Card } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `title` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders a bold title in the card header, giving the grouped content a clear heading at the top.
- `subtitle` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders a muted subtitle beneath the title to add supporting context or a brief secondary description.
- `actions` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Header actions rendered top-right, opposite the title/subtitle (e.g. a button or overflow menu).
- `footer` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders pinned footer content such as action buttons below the card body, separated from the main content.
- `variant` тАФ `"elevated" | "outline" | "soft"` ┬╖ default `"elevated"` тАФ Selects the surface treatment from elevated, outline, or soft to control how the card stands out from the page.
- `padding` тАФ `"none" | "md" | "lg"` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Sets the card's inner padding to none, md, or lg, with none useful for edge-to-edge media or custom layouts.
- `interactive` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ When true, adds a hover-lift effect and pointer cursor to signal the whole card is clickable.
- `fullHeight` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Stretches the card to fill its parent cell (height: 100%) so cards in a grid or flex row align to equal height.
- `children` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ The main body content of the card, rendered between the optional header and footer regions.
- `sx` тАФ `Sx` тАФ Style escape hatch: flat CSS goes inline (wins over base); nested selectors/at-rules like "&:hover" or "@media тАж" compile to a scoped stylesheet.

```jsx
import { Card } from "twico-ui";

<Card
  title="Monthly revenue"
  subtitle="June 2026"
  footer={<Button size="sm">View report</Button>}
>
  Revenue grew 18% month over month.
</Card>

<Card variant="outline" interactive>
  Hover me тАФ I lift.
</Card>
```

#### Carousel

Carousel is a sliding one-slide-per-view component with prev/next arrows, dot indicators, optional looping, and optional autoplay that pauses on hover. Use it to showcase a sequence of images or content panels where each child is a single slide.

`import { Carousel } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `showArrows` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ When true, displays prev/next arrow controls so users can step manually between slides in either direction.
- `showDots` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ When true, displays dot indicators beneath the slides showing position and letting users jump to a specific slide.
- `loop` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ When true, wraps navigation around the ends so advancing past the last slide returns to the first and vice versa.
- `autoPlay` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ When true, automatically advances slides on a timer, pausing while the pointer hovers over the carousel.
- `interval` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `4000` тАФ Sets the delay in milliseconds between automatic slide advances when autoPlay is enabled, defaulting to 4000.
- `children` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ The slide elements, where each direct child is rendered as a single full-width slide in the carousel.
- `index` тАФ `number` тАФ Controlled active slide index тАФ pair with onIndexChange.
- `defaultIndex` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `0` тАФ Initial slide index when uncontrolled.
- `onIndexChange` тАФ `(index: number) => void` тАФ Fired when the active slide changes (arrows, dots, or autoplay).

```jsx
import { Carousel } from "twico-ui";

<Carousel autoPlay interval={5000}>
  <img src="/a.jpg" alt="" />
  <img src="/b.jpg" alt="" />
  <div>Any slide content</div>
</Carousel>
```

#### Chart

A lightweight, dependency-free SVG chart that renders bar or line graphs (single or multi-series) with grid, axes, animated entrance, tooltips, and an optional legend. Use it to visualize small datasets like daily metrics or trends without pulling in a charting library.

`import { Chart } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `type` тАФ `"bar" | "line"` ┬╖ default `"bar"` тАФ Selects the rendering style, drawing the data as grouped bars or as connected lines for trends over time.
- `data` тАФ `ChartDatum[]` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Supplies the array of data points, each carrying a label plus one numeric field for every named series.
- `series` тАФ `string[]` ┬╖ default `["value"]` тАФ Names the numeric fields to plot, where multiple keys yield grouped bars or multiple lines per data point.
- `height` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `220` тАФ Sets the chart's pixel height, controlling its vertical footprint while it scales horizontally to its container.
- `showGrid` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Draws horizontal grid lines behind the data, on by default, to make values easier to read against the scale.
- `showAxis` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Renders axis tick marks and label text, enabled by default, so viewers can map plotted values to categories.
- `showLegend` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Displays a legend keying each series to its color, off by default and most useful when plotting multiple series.
- `valueFormat` тАФ `(value: number) => string` тАФ Formats raw numeric values into display strings for tooltips, letting you add units, separators, or currency symbols.
- `colors` тАФ `string[]` ┬╖ default `built-in token palette` тАФ Per-series colors (any CSS color), cycled when shorter than series.
- `ariaLabel` тАФ `string` тАФ Accessible name for the chart's <svg role="img">; defaults to a description of the chart type.

```jsx
import { Chart } from "twico-ui";

<Chart
  type="bar"
  data={[
    { label: "Mon", value: 240 },
    { label: "Tue", value: 310 },
    { label: "Wed", value: 280 },
  ]}
  valueFormat={(v) => `$${v}`}
/>

<Chart
  type="line"
  series={["signups", "active"]}
  showLegend
  data={[
    { label: "Jan", signups: 120, active: 80 },
    { label: "Feb", signups: 180, active: 140 },
  ]}
/>
```

#### Datatable

Datatable is an advanced, MUI Data Grid-style data table with sortable, filterable, hideable, pinnable, reorderable, and resizable columns, plus a toolbar, density control, multi-format export, optional checkbox selection with batch actions, an actions column, pagination, and a skeleton loading state. Use it to display large tabular datasets that need interactive sorting, filtering, grouping, aggregation, or a server-side mode where only the current page is loaded.

`import { Datatable } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `columns` тАФ `DatatableColumn array` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Defines each column's field, header, type, and behavior, controlling sorting, filtering, formatting, and pinning across the grid.
- `rows` тАФ `any array` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Supplies the row objects to render, holding only the current page's data when serverMode is enabled.
- `loading` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Replaces the grid body with skeleton rows while data is being fetched; off by default.
- `rowKey` тАФ `(row,index)=>key` ┬╖ default `(r,i)=>r.id??i` тАФ Returns a stable unique key per row, defaulting to the row's id or its index, to keep selection and renders consistent.
- `checkboxSelection` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Adds a leading checkbox column for selecting rows, enabling batch actions; off by default.
- `batchActions` тАФ `DatatableBatchAction array` ┬╖ default `[]` тАФ Defines actions shown in the selection toolbar that operate on the currently selected rows; empty by default.
- `density` тАФ `compact|standard|comfortable` ┬╖ default `comfortable` тАФ Sets the row height preset (compact, standard, or comfortable) to trade vertical space for readability, defaulting to comfortable.
- `pageSize` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `10` тАФ Rows per page (0 disables pagination). Uncontrolled by default (changing it re-applies + resets to page 0); controlled when onPageSizeChange is set.
- `page` тАФ `number` тАФ Controlled current page (0-based); when set, the table renders this page and reports changes via onPageChange.
- `onPageChange` тАФ `(page: number) => void` тАФ Fires with the next 0-based page on user pagination (and when a size/query change resets to page 0). Required to control page.
- `onPageSizeChange` тАФ `(pageSize: number) => void` тАФ Fires with the next rows-per-page when the user picks one; supplying it makes pageSize controlled.
- `pageSizeOptions` тАФ `number array` ┬╖ default `[5,10,25,50]` тАФ Lists the selectable rows-per-page values offered in the pagination control, defaulting to [5,10,25,50].
- `height` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `440` тАФ Sets the maximum scroll height of the grid body in pixels before rows scroll internally, defaulting to 440.
- `serverMode` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Switches to server-side data handling so sorting, filtering, and paging are delegated to your backend; off by default.
- `rowCount` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `-` тАФ Tells the grid the total number of server-side rows so pagination can render correctly in serverMode.
- `onServerChange` тАФ `(state)=>void` ┬╖ default `-` тАФ Fires with the current query state (sort, filter, page) in serverMode so you can fetch the matching rows.
- `showExport` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Shows the toolbar Export button (CSV split button with a format menu); hidden by default тАФ enable it to expose exporting.
- `showDensity` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Shows the toolbar density button that cycles compact/standard/comfortable; hidden by default (the density prop still sets the row height).
- `showPivot` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Shows the toolbar Pivot button; hidden by default, but appears automatically when a pivot model or pivotMode is provided.
- `exportFilename` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `export` тАФ Sets the base filename used for exported files, defaulting to "export".
- `aggregationValues` тАФ `object|null` ┬╖ default `null` тАФ Supplies precomputed footer aggregation values for serverMode, where totals cannot be calculated client-side; null by default.
- `disableColumnReorder` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Prevents users from dragging columns into a new order, locking the column arrangement; off by default.
- `emptyMessage` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Message shown when there are no rows; the default is filter-aware ("No rows match your filters" vs "No rows").
- `renderEmpty` тАФ `() => React.ReactNode` тАФ Render a custom empty state inside the table body (e.g. the shipped EmptyState); overrides emptyMessage.
- `disableColumnResize` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Prevents resizing columns, locking their widths; off by default. When enabled, drag a header's right edge to resize, or double-click it to auto-fit the column to its content (Excel-style).
- `editMode` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Makes all columns editable inline so users can change cell values directly; off by default.
- `onRowUpdate` тАФ `(updated,original,field)=>void` ┬╖ default `-` тАФ Fires when an inline cell edit is committed, receiving the updated row, the original row, and the edited field.
- `onRowsChange` тАФ `(rows)=>void` ┬╖ default `-` тАФ Fires with the full next array of rows after an edit, letting you persist the new dataset.
- `onBatchUpdate` тАФ `(rows,patch,keys)=>void` ┬╖ default `-` тАФ Fires when a batch edit is applied, receiving the affected rows, the patch object, and the selected keys.
- `showPageJumper` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Shows a go-to-page input in the pagination footer for jumping directly to a page number; enabled by default.
- `selectionMode` тАФ `none|row|cell` ┬╖ default `none` тАФ Sets click-to-select granularity (none, row, or cell), controlling what gets highlighted on click, defaulting to none.
- `onRowClick` тАФ `(row,key)=>void` ┬╖ default `-` тАФ Fires with the clicked row and its key, useful for navigation or opening a detail view.
- `onCellClick` тАФ `(value,row,field)=>void` ┬╖ default `-` тАФ Fires with the clicked cell's value, its row, and the field name when a cell is clicked.
- `onActiveCellChange` тАФ `(cell|null)=>void` ┬╖ default `-` тАФ Fires when the active cell changes via keyboard or click, receiving the new cell descriptor or null.
- `showAggregation` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Shows the toolbar Aggregation button and starts with the totals row on; hidden by default, so enable it to expose column aggregations.
- `ariaLabel` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `Data table` тАФ Sets the grid's accessible aria-label for screen readers, defaulting to "Data table".
- `rowGrouping` тАФ `string array` ┬╖ default `[]` тАФ Lists the field names to group rows by on initial render, collapsing rows into expandable groups; empty by default.
- `rowPinning` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Allows users to pin rows to the top or bottom so they stay visible while scrolling; off by default.
- `rowReorder` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Enables drag-and-drop reordering of rows so users can change their sequence; off by default.
- `rowResize` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Allows users to drag row edges to adjust individual row heights; off by default.
- `onRowOrderChange` тАФ `(keys)=>void` ┬╖ default `-` тАФ Fires with the new array of row keys after a drag reorder, letting you persist the updated order.
- `pivot` тАФ `object|null` ┬╖ default `null` тАФ Supplies the initial pivot model defining row, column, and value fields for cross-tabulated analysis; null by default.
- `pivotMode` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Renders the grid in pivot view from the start instead of the flat table layout; off by default.
- `align` тАФ `"left" | "right"` ┬╖ default `"right" for number/actions columns, else "left"` тАФ Cell alignment; currently affects the actions column's button justification. Already supported at runtime тАФ newly exposed in the .d.ts.
- `virtualized` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Opt into row virtualization (windowing). Active only when pagination is off (pageSize={0}) and row grouping is not active; otherwise ignored and the table renders normally. Pair with a fixed height. Selection/inline-edit keep working because they key off the row id, not the rendered index.
- `overscan` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `8` тАФ Extra rows rendered above and below the visible window for smoother fast scrolling when virtualized.
- `rowHeight` тАФ `number` тАФ Estimated/fixed row height in px used to compute the virtualization window. Set if rows have a custom height so the scrollbar stays accurate.

```jsx
import { useState } from "react";
import { Datatable, Avatar, Badge } from "twico-ui";

// Sample data тАФ swap for your own array or API rows. Each row needs a stable `id`.
const seed = [
  { id: 1, name: "Ava Stone", email: "ava.stone@twico.io", role: "Admin", department: "Engineering", status: "active", country: "US", seats: 12, mrr: 480, salary: 128000, startDate: "2023-01-14" },
  { id: 2, name: "Liam Reed", email: "liam.reed@twico.io", role: "Editor", department: "Design", status: "invited", country: "GB", seats: 4, mrr: 120, salary: 92000, startDate: "2023-05-02" },
  { id: 3, name: "Noah Park", email: "noah.park@twico.io", role: "Viewer", department: "Sales", status: "active", country: "DE", seats: 2, mrr: 60, salary: 74000, startDate: "2024-02-20" },
  { id: 4, name: "Mia Cruz", email: "mia.cruz@twico.io", role: "Editor", department: "Support", status: "suspended", country: "ID", seats: 7, mrr: 240, salary: 105000, startDate: "2022-11-09" },
];

const ROLES = ["Admin", "Editor", "Viewer"];
const DEPARTMENTS = ["Engineering", "Design", "Sales", "Marketing", "Support", "Finance"];
const STATUSES = ["active", "invited", "suspended"];
const STATUS_TONE = { active: "success", invited: "info", suspended: "danger" };
const usd = (n) => "$" + Number(n).toLocaleString("en-US");

// Inline SVG icons (no CDN) for the actions column + batch toolbar.
const Eye = () => (<svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="2"><path d="M1 12s4-8 11-8 11 8 11 8-4 8-11 8-11-8-11-8z" /><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="3" /></svg>);
const Pencil = () => (<svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="2"><path d="M12 20h9" /><path d="M16.5 3.5a2.1 2.1 0 0 1 3 3L7 19l-4 1 1-4z" /></svg>);
const Trash = () => (<svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="2"><path d="M3 6h18M8 6V4h8v2M19 6l-1 14H6L5 6" /></svg>);
const Download = () => (<svg width="15" height="15" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="2"><path d="M12 3v12M7 10l5 5 5-5M5 21h14" /></svg>);
const Mail = () => (<svg width="15" height="15" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="2"><path d="M3 6h18v12H3z" /><path d="M3 7l9 6 9-6" /></svg>);

export default function Example() {
  const [rows, setRows] = useState(seed);

  const columns = [
    {
      field: "name", headerName: "Name", width: 220, pinned: "left",
      renderCell: (v) => (
        <span style={{ display: "flex", alignItems: "center", gap: 10 }}>
          <Avatar name={v} size="sm" /> <strong>{v}</strong>
        </span>
      ),
    },
    { field: "email", headerName: "Email", width: 220 },

    // Editable columns: double-click a cell to edit it, AND when rows are ticked an
    // "Edit" button appears in the selection toolbar to set one value across every
    // selected row at once (fires onBatchUpdate).
    {
      field: "role", headerName: "Role", width: 130,
      editable: true, editType: "select", valueOptions: ROLES,
      renderCell: (v) => <Badge variant="soft" size="sm" tone="neutral">{v}</Badge>,
    },
    { field: "department", headerName: "Department", width: 150, editable: true, editType: "select", valueOptions: DEPARTMENTS },
    {
      field: "status", headerName: "Status", width: 130,
      editable: true, editType: "select", valueOptions: STATUSES,
      renderCell: (v) => <Badge variant="soft" size="sm" tone={STATUS_TONE[v]}>{v}</Badge>,
    },
    { field: "country", headerName: "Country", width: 120, valueOptions: ["US", "GB", "DE", "ID", "JP", "BR", "CA", "AU"] },
    { field: "seats", headerName: "Seats", type: "number", width: 110, editable: true, aggregation: "sum" },
    { field: "mrr", headerName: "MRR", type: "number", width: 120, aggregation: "sum", valueFormatter: usd, aggregationFormatter: usd },
    { field: "salary", headerName: "Salary", type: "number", width: 130, editable: true, aggregation: "avg", valueFormatter: usd },
    { field: "startDate", headerName: "Start date", width: 130 },

    // Per-row actions: inline icon buttons + an overflow (тЛо) menu (showInMenu).
    {
      field: "actions", headerName: "Actions", type: "actions", width: 110,
      getActions: (row) => [
        { icon: <Eye />, label: "View", onClick: () => console.log("view", row.id) },
        { icon: <Pencil />, label: "Edit", showInMenu: true, onClick: () => console.log("edit", row.id) },
        { icon: <Trash />, label: "Delete", showInMenu: true, danger: true, onClick: () => setRows((d) => d.filter((r) => r.id !== row.id)) },
      ],
    },
  ];

  // Actions for the toolbar that replaces the header when one or more rows are ticked.
  // (A built-in "Edit" button is added automatically because some columns are editable.)
  const batchActions = [
    { label: "Export", icon: <Download />, onClick: (keys, selected, clear) => clear() },
    { label: "Email", icon: <Mail />, onClick: (keys, selected, clear) => { console.log(selected); clear(); } },
    { label: "Delete", icon: <Trash />, danger: true, onClick: (keys, selected, clear) => { setRows((d) => d.filter((r) => !keys.includes(r.id))); clear(); } },
  ];

  return (
    <Datatable
      columns={columns}
      rows={rows}
      checkboxSelection
      rowNumbers
      batchActions={batchActions}
      onRowUpdate={(updated) => setRows((d) => d.map((r) => (r.id === updated.id ? updated : r)))}
      onBatchUpdate={(changed) => setRows((d) => d.map((r) => changed.find((c) => c.id === r.id) || r))}
      showAggregation
      showDensity
      showExport
      rowPinning
      pageSize={10}
    />
  );
}
```

#### DonutChart

A PieChart preset rendered as a ring with a hollow center, showing proportional slices of a single value series with tooltips, an optional legend, and a donut center label. Reach for it to visualize parts-of-a-whole (traffic sources, budget splits, storage breakdowns) when you want a total called out in the middle.

`import { DonutChart } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `data` тАФ `PieChartDatum[]` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Slices to plot; each is a { label, value, color? } where only positive values contribute a slice.
- `donut` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Render as a donut (ring) instead of a filled pie; on by default for this preset, pass false for a solid pie.
- `innerRadius` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `0.6` тАФ Inner-hole radius as a fraction (0..1) of the outer radius; 0.6 when donut, else 0.
- `startAngle` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `0` тАФ Angle in degrees, clockwise from 12 o'clock, where the first slice starts.
- `padAngle` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `0` тАФ Gap in degrees inserted between adjacent slices to visually separate them.
- `showLabels` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Draw a percentage label centered on each non-hairline slice directly on the ring.
- `showLegend` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Show the slice legend below the chart, mapping each color to its label.
- `centerLabel` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Content for the donut's center; defaults to the formatted total when donut, and is ignored for a solid pie.
- `valueFormat` тАФ `(value: number) => string` ┬╖ default `toLocaleString` тАФ Formatter applied to tooltip and hidden data-table values, e.g. adding a currency or percent sign.
- `height` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `260` тАФ Pixel height of the chart; the square chart area is sized from this value.
- `colors` тАФ `string[]` тАФ Per-slice colors (any CSS color), cycled when shorter than data; defaults to the built-in token palette.
- `ariaLabel` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"donut chart"` тАФ Accessible name for the <svg role="img">; per-slice values live in the hidden data table, not this label.
- `tableFallback` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Render a visually-hidden data table as an accessible text alternative to the SVG (WCAG 1.1.1).
- `caption` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Caption for the hidden data table; defaults to the chart's accessible label.

```jsx
import { DonutChart } from "twico-ui";

<DonutChart
  data={[
    { label: "Direct", value: 42 },
    { label: "Referral", value: 28 },
    { label: "Social", value: 30 },
  ]}
  centerLabel="Traffic"
  showLegend
/>

<DonutChart data={data} donut={false} />
```

#### FunnelChart

A dependency-free inline-SVG funnel chart that draws descending stages as centered trapezoids tapering from each stage's value to the next. Reach for it to visualize conversion or drop-off flows where each step is a subset of the one above.

`import { FunnelChart } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `data` тАФ `FunnelDatum[]` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Funnel stages, each `{ label, value, color? }`, ordered from the widest/top stage down to the narrowest.
- `showValues` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Print each stage's formatted value in its centered caption.
- `showPercent` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Print each stage's percentage of the first (top) stage in its caption.
- `horizontal` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Lay the funnel out leftтЖТright (tapering vertically) instead of the default topтЖТbottom flow.
- `gap` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `2` тАФ Pixel gap between adjacent stage bands.
- `height` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `300` тАФ Pixel height of the chart.
- `colors` тАФ `string[]` тАФ Per-stage colors (any CSS color), cycled when shorter than data; defaults to the token palette.
- `valueFormat` тАФ `(value: number) => string` ┬╖ default `toLocaleString` тАФ Formatter for captions, tooltips, and the hidden data-table cells.
- `ariaLabel` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"funnel chart"` тАФ Accessible name for the chart's `<svg role="img">`; also accepts `aria-label`, with per-stage values in the hidden table.
- `tableFallback` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Render a visually-hidden data table as an accessible text alternative (WCAG 1.1.1).
- `caption` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Caption for the hidden data table; defaults to the chart's accessible label.

```jsx
import { FunnelChart } from "twico-ui";

<FunnelChart
  data={[
    { label: "Visits", value: 8200 },
    { label: "Signups", value: 4100 },
    { label: "Trials", value: 1600 },
    { label: "Paid", value: 540 },
  ]}
  valueFormat={(v) => v.toLocaleString()}
/>

<FunnelChart horizontal showPercent={false} height={220} data={[
  { label: "Leads", value: 500, color: "var(--sky-500)" },
  { label: "Qualified", value: 320 },
  { label: "Won", value: 90 },
]} />
```

#### Gauge

A dependency-free inline-SVG radial gauge that renders a single value as a thick arc sweeping between two angles, with the value and an optional caption in the center. Reach for it to show a KPI, progress, or capacity reading тАФ or pass `series` to draw several concentric rings as a radial-bar chart.

`import { Gauge } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `value` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `0` тАФ The value to display, mapped from the min..max domain onto the arc sweep.
- `min` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `0` тАФ Lower bound of the value domain, mapped to the startAngle.
- `max` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `100` тАФ Upper bound of the value domain, mapped to the endAngle.
- `startAngle` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `-110` тАФ Sweep start angle in degrees, where 0 is the top and angles run clockwise.
- `endAngle` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `110` тАФ Sweep end angle in degrees, where 0 is the top and angles run clockwise.
- `thickness` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `0.16` тАФ Ring stroke width expressed as a fraction of the gauge radius.
- `showValue` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Show the formatted value in the center; applies to a single gauge only.
- `label` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Small caption rendered under the center value for a single gauge.
- `valueFormat` тАФ `(value: number) => string` тАФ Formats the center value and hidden-table cells; defaults to a rounded integer.
- `color` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"var(--color-primary)"` тАФ Value-arc color, accepting any CSS color string.
- `trackColor` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"var(--color-surface-sunken)"` тАФ Background track color behind the value arc, accepting any CSS color string.
- `size` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `200` тАФ SVG width and height in pixels; the gauge is always square.
- `series` тАФ `GaugeSeries[]` тАФ Multiple concentric radial bars; when set, the single value and label are ignored.
- `showLegend` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Show the legend for a multi-ring gauge driven by the series prop.
- `ariaLabel` тАФ `string` тАФ Accessible name for the SVG role="img"; defaults to a value summary and also accepts aria-label.
- `tableFallback` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Render a visually-hidden data table as an accessible text alternative per WCAG 1.1.1.
- `caption` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Caption for the hidden data table; defaults to the gauge's accessible label.

```jsx
import { Gauge } from "twico-ui";

<Gauge value={72} label="CPU" valueFormat={(n) => `${Math.round(n)}%`} />

<Gauge size={220} series={[
  { value: 82, label: "Complete", color: "var(--emerald-500)" },
  { value: 54, label: "In review" },
  { value: 23, label: "Blocked" },
]} />
```

#### Heatmap

A dependency-free SVG matrix of colored cells keyed by (x, y), each shaded by a single-hue intensity scale between min and max. Reach for it to reveal density, correlation, or activity patterns across two categorical dimensions тАФ think punch-card activity grids, cohort retention, or correlation matrices.

`import { Heatmap } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `data` тАФ `HeatmapDatum[]` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Cells to plot as { x, y, value } objects; X columns and Y rows are derived from these in first-seen order.
- `min` тАФ `number` тАФ Low end of the color scale; defaults to the minimum value found in data.
- `max` тАФ `number` тАФ High end of the color scale; defaults to the maximum value found in data.
- `colorScale` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"var(--brand-500)"` тАФ Base token color for the high end; cells mix it over transparent by their value intensity.
- `showValues` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Print each cell's formatted value at its center in addition to the color shading.
- `cellGap` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `2` тАФ Gap in pixels between adjacent cells in the grid.
- `radius` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `2` тАФ Cell corner radius in pixels.
- `xLabel` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Optional title rendered beneath the X-axis category labels.
- `yLabel` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Optional title rendered rotated alongside the Y-axis category labels.
- `showLegend` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Show the low-to-high gradient scale legend below the grid.
- `valueFormat` тАФ `(value: number) => string` ┬╖ default `fmtNumber` тАФ Formatter for tooltips, printed cell values, and the hidden data table.
- `height` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `300` тАФ Pixel height of the chart.
- `ariaLabel` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"heatmap"` тАФ Accessible name for the chart's SVG role="img"; also accepts aria-label, with per-cell values exposed via the hidden table.
- `tableFallback` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Render a visually-hidden data table as an accessible text alternative (WCAG 1.1.1).
- `caption` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Caption for the hidden data table; defaults to the chart's accessible label.

```jsx
import { Heatmap } from "twico-ui";

<Heatmap
  xLabel="Hour"
  yLabel="Day"
  data={[
    { x: "9a", y: "Mon", value: 12 }, { x: "12p", y: "Mon", value: 48 },
    { x: "9a", y: "Tue", value: 20 }, { x: "12p", y: "Tue", value: 62 },
  ]}
/>

<Heatmap showValues colorScale="var(--emerald-500)" valueFormat={(v) => `${v}%`} data={cells} />
```

#### Kanban

Kanban is a drag-and-drop board that arranges cards into columns you can drag between. Use it for visualizing and reorganizing work items (tasks, tickets, stages) by status, either controlled via cards + onCardMove or uncontrolled.

`import { Kanban } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `columns` тАФ `KanbanColumn[] ({ id: string; title: React.ReactNode; color?: string })` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Defines the board columns, each with an id matching cards' column field, a title, and an optional heading dot color.
- `cards` тАФ `KanbanCard[] ({ id; column; title?; description?; tags?; footer?; ... })` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Supplies the cards to render, each assigned to a column via its column field and carrying title, description, and tags.
- `onCardMove` тАФ `(cardId: string, toColumn: string, nextCards: KanbanCard[]) => void` тАФ Fires when a card is dropped into a different column, passing the card id, target column, and updated card list.
- `renderCard` тАФ `(card: KanbanCard) => React.ReactNode` тАФ Overrides the default card contents with a custom renderer, falling back to title, description, tags, and footer.
- `defaultCards` тАФ `KanbanCard[]` ┬╖ default `[]` тАФ Initial cards when uncontrolled (the board applies moves internally).

```jsx
import { Kanban } from "twico-ui";

<Kanban
  columns={[
    { id: "todo", title: "To do", color: "var(--slate-400)" },
    { id: "doing", title: "In progress", color: "var(--amber-500)" },
    { id: "done", title: "Done", color: "var(--emerald-500)" },
  ]}
  cards={[
    { id: "1", column: "todo", title: "Design tokens", tags: ["Design"] },
    { id: "2", column: "doing", title: "Datatable filters" },
    { id: "3", column: "done", title: "Ship release" },
  ]}
  onCardMove={(id, to) => {}}
/>
```

#### List

List renders a vertical stack of rows with optional leading/trailing slots, a title, and a description. Use it for compact data displays like people, files, or settings, where rows can be static, links, or buttons.

`import { List } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `items` тАФ `ListItemData[]` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Provides the rows to render, each with a title plus optional description, leading, trailing, onClick, href, and active fields.
- `plain` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Drops the card chrome to render borderless, transparent rows when set to true; defaults to false for the boxed style.
- `emptyMessage` тАФ `React.ReactNode` ┬╖ default `"Nothing here yet"` тАФ Shown as a centered zero-state row when items is empty, so an empty list reads as intentional rather than broken.

```jsx
import { List } from "twico-ui";

<List
  items={[
    { title: "Ada Park", description: "ada@twico.dev", trailing: "Active", onClick: () => {} },
    { title: "report.pdf", description: "2.4 MB", trailing: "Today" },
  ]}
/>
```

#### Pagination

Pagination renders page navigation with prev/next buttons and smart ellipsis truncation, keeping boundary and sibling pages visible. Use it to let users move through paged data (tables, lists, search results), optionally with a "Go to" page jumper.

`import { Pagination } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `page` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `1` тАФ The currently active 1-based page number, used to highlight the active button and compute which pages to show.
- `total` тАФ `number` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Total number of pages available, determining the range of page buttons and when ellipsis truncation appears.
- `onChange` тАФ `(page: number) => void` тАФ Fires with the destination page number whenever the user clicks a page, prev, next, or jumper control.
- `siblings` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `1` тАФ Number of adjacent page buttons rendered on each side of the current page before ellipsis truncation kicks in.
- `boundaries` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `1` тАФ Number of page buttons always kept visible at each end so the first and last pages stay reachable.
- `size` тАФ `"sm" | "md"` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Sets the control density, sm or md, scaling button height and spacing to match the surrounding layout.
- `showJumper` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Adds a "Go to" number input so users can jump directly to a specific page instead of clicking through.
- `jumperLabel` тАФ `React.ReactNode` ┬╖ default `"Go to"` тАФ Text or node rendered before the jumper input, defaulting to "Go to", to clarify what the field does.
- `getPageLabel` тАФ `(page: number) => string` ┬╖ default `(n) => `Page ${n}`` тАФ Accessible label for each numbered page button, so screen readers announce "Page 3" rather than a bare number; override for i18n.
- `defaultPage` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `1` тАФ Initial 1-based page when uncontrolled (no `page` prop).
- `showPageJumper` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Show a "Go to" page input for jumping directly to a page; preferred alias for showJumper.

```jsx
import { Pagination } from "twico-ui";

const [page, setPage] = useState(1);

<Pagination
  page={page}
  total={12}
  onChange={setPage}
  showJumper
/>
```

#### PieChart

A dependency-free inline-SVG pie or donut chart that renders proportional slices of a whole from a single value series, with per-slice tooltips, an optional legend, percentage labels, and a donut center label. Reach for it to show part-to-whole composition; use Chart for bar/line/area trends.

`import { PieChart } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `data` тАФ `PieChartDatum[]` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Slices to plot, each a { label, value, color? }; only positive values contribute a slice and negatives are treated as zero.
- `donut` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Render as a donut ring instead of a filled pie, using the default innerRadius.
- `innerRadius` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `0.6 when donut, else 0` тАФ Inner-hole radius as a fraction (0..1) of the outer radius, letting you tune the ring thickness.
- `startAngle` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `0` тАФ Angle in degrees, clockwise from 12 o'clock, where the first slice begins.
- `padAngle` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `0` тАФ Gap in degrees inserted between adjacent slices to visually separate them.
- `showLabels` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Draw a percentage label centered on each non-hairline slice.
- `showLegend` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Show the slice legend below the chart.
- `centerLabel` тАФ `React.ReactNode` ┬╖ default `formatted total when donut` тАФ Content for a donut's center; defaults to the formatted total and is ignored for a solid pie.
- `valueFormat` тАФ `(value: number) => string` ┬╖ default `toLocaleString` тАФ Formatter for the tooltip and hidden data-table values.
- `height` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `260` тАФ Pixel height of the chart; the square chart area is sized from this value.
- `colors` тАФ `string[]` ┬╖ default `built-in token palette` тАФ Per-slice colors (any CSS color), cycled when shorter than data; defaults to the built-in token palette.
- `ariaLabel` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"pie chart" / "donut chart"` тАФ Accessible name for the role="img" SVG; per-slice values live in the hidden data table, not this label.
- `tableFallback` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Render a visually-hidden data table as an accessible text alternative for the chart (WCAG 1.1.1).
- `caption` тАФ `React.ReactNode` ┬╖ default `chart's accessible label` тАФ Caption for the hidden data table; defaults to the chart's accessible label.

```jsx
import { PieChart } from "twico-ui";

<PieChart data={[
  { label: "Direct", value: 340 },
  { label: "Referral", value: 210 },
  { label: "Social", value: 120 },
]} />

<PieChart donut showLabels centerLabel="100%" data={[
  { label: "Done", value: 62, color: "var(--emerald-500)" },
  { label: "In progress", value: 28 },
  { label: "Blocked", value: 10 },
]} />
```

#### PolarAreaChart

A dependency-free inline-SVG polar-area (Coxcomb / Nightingale rose) chart of equal-angle slices whose radius encodes each value over concentric value rings. Reach for it to compare a handful of categories where slice area тАФ scaled by the square root so it stays statistically honest тАФ reads more naturally than bars or wedges.

`import { PolarAreaChart } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `data` тАФ `PolarAreaDatum[]` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Data points тАФ one equal-angle slice each, ordered clockwise from startAngle, each with a label, value, and optional color.
- `max` тАФ `number` тАФ Radial maximum (the value at the outer ring); defaults to a nice ceiling of the largest value.
- `levels` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `4` тАФ Number of concentric grid rings drawn, which also sets how many value tick labels appear.
- `startAngle` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `0` тАФ Angle in degrees of the first slice's leading edge, measured clockwise from the 12 o'clock position.
- `showLegend` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Show the per-slice legend listing each category label with its swatch below the chart.
- `valueFormat` тАФ `(value: number) => string` ┬╖ default `fmtNumber` тАФ Formats values shown in the per-slice tooltip and the hidden data-table cells.
- `height` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `280` тАФ Pixel height of the chart; the square plotting area is centered within the full-width container.
- `colors` тАФ `string[]` тАФ Per-slice colors (any CSS color) cycled across slices; defaults to the built-in token palette.
- `ariaLabel` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"polar area chart"` тАФ Accessible name for the chart's SVG role="img"; aria-label is also accepted as an alternative.
- `tableFallback` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Render a visually-hidden data table as an accessible text alternative for screen readers (WCAG 1.1.1).
- `caption` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Caption for the hidden data table; defaults to the chart's accessible label when omitted.

```jsx
import { PolarAreaChart } from "twico-ui";

<PolarAreaChart
  data={[
    { label: "Mon", value: 12 },
    { label: "Tue", value: 30 },
    { label: "Wed", value: 22 },
    { label: "Thu", value: 41 },
    { label: "Fri", value: 18, color: "var(--rose-500)" },
  ]}
  valueFormat={(v) => `${v}h`}
/>

<PolarAreaChart max={100} levels={5} startAngle={-90} showLegend={false} data={[
  { label: "N", value: 40 }, { label: "E", value: 65 }, { label: "S", value: 25 }, { label: "W", value: 80 },
]} />
```

#### RadarChart

A dependency-free SVG radar (spider) chart that plots one or more series as closed polygons over a shared set of categorical axes radiating from a center. Reach for it to compare multi-dimensional profiles тАФ skill sets, scorecards, feature ratings тАФ where each entity is a shape across the same handful of metrics.

`import { RadarChart } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `data` тАФ `RadarDatum<K>[]` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Data rows, one per axis; each carries a label plus one numeric field for every series key you plot.
- `series` тАФ `K[]` ┬╖ default `["value"]` тАФ Series field names to draw as polygons; supplying more than one overlays several shapes on the same axes.
- `max` тАФ `number` тАФ Radial value at the outer ring; defaults to a nice ceiling computed from the largest datum.
- `levels` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `4` тАФ Number of concentric grid rings drawn between the center and the outer edge of the chart.
- `fill` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Fills each polygon at low opacity in addition to its stroke; set false for a clean outline.
- `showLegend` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `series.length > 1` тАФ Shows a series legend; defaults to true when more than one series is plotted, otherwise false.
- `showDots` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Draws a marker dot at each polygon vertex where an axis meets its series value.
- `colors` тАФ `string[]` тАФ Per-series CSS colors, cycled when shorter than series; defaults to the built-in design-token palette.
- `valueFormat` тАФ `(value: number) => string` тАФ Formats values shown in tooltips and the hidden accessible data-table cells.
- `height` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `300` тАФ Pixel height of the chart, which renders on a square canvas of this size.
- `ariaLabel` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"radar chart"` тАФ Accessible name for the chart's role="img" SVG; also accepts the aria-label attribute.
- `tableFallback` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Renders a visually-hidden data table as a text alternative to the SVG for screen readers (WCAG 1.1.1).
- `caption` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Caption for the hidden data table; defaults to the chart's accessible label.

```jsx
import { RadarChart } from "twico-ui";

<RadarChart
  series={["team", "rival"]}
  showLegend
  data={[
    { label: "Speed", team: 80, rival: 65 },
    { label: "Power", team: 60, rival: 90 },
    { label: "Defense", team: 85, rival: 60 },
  ]}
/>

<RadarChart series={["score"]} max={100} levels={5} data={scoreData} />
```

#### RangeChart

A dependency-free inline-SVG chart for values that span a minтЖТmax range: horizontal range bars for a timeline/Gantt of one band per row, or a range area shading the band between a per-category min line and max line. Reach for it when each data point is an interval (startтЖТend, lowтЖТhigh) rather than a single value.

`import { RangeChart } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `type` тАФ `"bar" | "area"` ┬╖ default `"bar"` тАФ Chooses horizontal range bars (a timeline/Gantt) or a range area shading the band between a min and max line.
- `data` тАФ `RangeDatum[]` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Range points, each a { label, min, max, color? }; bars run topтЖТbottom while the area runs leftтЖТright.
- `height` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `300` тАФ Pixel height of the chart; the SVG stretches to fill 100% of its container width.
- `showGrid` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Toggles the value grid lines тАФ vertical for bars, horizontal for the area band.
- `showAxis` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Toggles the value axis together with the per-category labels drawn alongside the chart.
- `colors` тАФ `string[]` тАФ Palette for bar fills and the area band, cycled by index; defaults to the built-in token palette.
- `valueFormat` тАФ `(value: number) => string` ┬╖ default `toLocaleString` тАФ Formats numbers in tooltips and the hidden data-table cells, e.g. adding units or currency symbols.
- `ariaLabel` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"range <type> chart"` тАФ Accessible name for the chart's role="img" SVG; aria-label is also accepted as an alias.
- `tableFallback` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Renders a visually-hidden min/max data table as an accessible text alternative for screen readers (WCAG 1.1.1).
- `caption` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Caption for the hidden data table; defaults to the chart's accessible label when omitted.

```jsx
import { RangeChart } from "twico-ui";

<RangeChart type="bar" data={[
  { label: "Design", min: 0, max: 4 },
  { label: "Build", min: 3, max: 9 },
]} />

<RangeChart type="area" data={[
  { label: "Mon", min: 12, max: 20 },
  { label: "Tue", min: 14, max: 24 },
]} />
```

#### ScatterChart

A dependency-free inline-SVG scatter and bubble chart that plots x/y points across two nice-scaled numeric axes, with one or many series, grid, tooltips, an optional legend, and a hidden data table. Reach for it to show correlation or distribution between two numeric variables тАФ add a per-point z value to encode a third dimension as bubble size.

`import { ScatterChart } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `series` тАФ `ScatterSeries[]` ┬╖ **required** тАФ The series to plot; each has an optional name/color and a list of { x, y, z?, label? } points.
- `bubble` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Size the dots by their z value (bubble chart); auto-enabled when any point has a numeric z.
- `maxBubble` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `26` тАФ Maximum bubble radius in pixels тАФ the largest z value maps to this size.
- `dotRadius` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `4.5` тАФ Fixed dot radius in pixels used when the chart is not in bubble mode.
- `height` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `300` тАФ Pixel height of the chart, which also sets the SVG aspect ratio so dots stay round.
- `xLabel` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Axis title drawn beneath the x axis.
- `yLabel` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Axis title drawn rotated beside the y axis.
- `showGrid` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Whether to draw the background grid lines behind the plotted points.
- `showLegend` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true when >1 series` тАФ Whether to show the series legend; defaults on when there is more than one series.
- `xFormat` тАФ `(value: number) => string` ┬╖ default `valueFormat` тАФ Formatter for x values in tooltips and the hidden table; defaults to valueFormat.
- `yFormat` тАФ `(value: number) => string` ┬╖ default `valueFormat` тАФ Formatter for y values in tooltips and the hidden table; defaults to valueFormat.
- `valueFormat` тАФ `(value: number) => string` ┬╖ default `locale-grouped numbers` тАФ Fallback value formatter for x, y, and z values; defaults to locale-grouped numbers.
- `ariaLabel` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"scatter chart" / "bubble chart"` тАФ Accessible name for the chart's role="img" SVG; also accepts aria-label. Per-point values live in the hidden table.
- `tableFallback` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Render a visually-hidden data table as an accessible text alternative to the SVG (WCAG 1.1.1).
- `caption` тАФ `React.ReactNode` ┬╖ default `chart's accessible label` тАФ Caption for the hidden data table; defaults to the chart's accessible label.

```jsx
import { ScatterChart } from "twico-ui";

<ScatterChart
  xLabel="Weight"
  yLabel="Score"
  series={[
    { name: "Group A", points: [{ x: 12, y: 30 }, { x: 20, y: 45 }] },
    { name: "Group B", points: [{ x: 15, y: 22 }, { x: 28, y: 38 }] },
  ]}
/>

// Bubble chart тАФ a numeric z auto-enables sizing
<ScatterChart
  maxBubble={32}
  series={[{ name: "Markets", points: [
    { x: 40, y: 12, z: 900, label: "US" },
    { x: 22, y: 30, z: 400, label: "EU" },
  ] }]}
/>
```

#### Sparkline

A minimal, word-sized trend chart тАФ line, area, or bars тАФ with no axes, grid, ticks, or legend. Reach for it to sit inline beside text or inside a table cell or stat tile; for a full chart with axes, tooltips, and a legend use Chart instead.

`import { Sparkline } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `data` тАФ `SparklineDatum[]` ┬╖ **required** тАФ The points to plot: bare numbers, or `{ value, label }` objects whose label is used for tooltips and the hidden table.
- `type` тАФ `"line" | "area" | "bar"` ┬╖ default `"line"` тАФ Trend style: `area` fills below the line and `bar` draws thin columns instead of a continuous line.
- `area` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Shorthand for `type="area"`, filling the region below the line without setting the full type prop.
- `color` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"var(--color-primary)"` тАФ Stroke and fill color; accepts any CSS color, including a design token like `var(--emerald-500)`.
- `width` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `120` тАФ The SVG viewbox width in pixels; the rendered element still scales to fill its container width.
- `height` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `40` тАФ Pixel height of the sparkline, controlling how tall the trend renders inline.
- `strokeWidth` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `2` тАФ Line stroke width in pixels; ignored for the bar type, which draws columns rather than a stroke.
- `curve` тАФ `"straight" | "smooth" | "stepped"` ┬╖ default `"smooth"` тАФ Line and area interpolation: straight segments, a smooth spline, or stepped right-angle transitions between points.
- `showDots` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Emphasize the last data point with a filled dot to highlight the most recent value.
- `min` тАФ `number` тАФ Pin the low end of the value scale; when omitted it is derived from the data's minimum.
- `max` тАФ `number` тАФ Pin the high end of the value scale; when omitted it is derived from the data's maximum.
- `valueFormat` тАФ `(value: number) => string` ┬╖ default `toLocaleString` тАФ Formatter for values shown in tooltips and the hidden data table, e.g. adding a currency or percent unit.
- `ariaLabel` тАФ `string` тАФ Accessible name for the `<svg role="img">`; defaults to a spoken summary of point count and first/last/min/max.
- `tableFallback` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Render a visually-hidden data table as a WCAG 1.1.1 text alternative; off by default since the aria-label usually suffices.
- `caption` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Caption for the hidden data table; defaults to the accessible label when not provided.

```jsx
import { Sparkline } from "twico-ui";

<Sparkline data={[3, 5, 4, 8, 6, 9, 7, 12]} showDots />
<Sparkline area color="var(--emerald-500)" data={[120, 180, 140, 220]} />
<Sparkline type="bar" height={28} data={[4, 6, 2, 8, 5, 7]} />
```

#### Stat

Stat is a KPI / metric card that displays a label, a large value, and optional icon, trend delta, and help text. Use it on dashboards or summary panels to surface a single key metric with its trend direction.

`import { Stat } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` тАФ `React.ReactNode` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Renders the metric's caption shown above the value, naming what the number represents such as "Revenue".
- `value` тАФ `React.ReactNode` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Renders the large primary metric value that anchors the card, like a count, currency amount, or percentage.
- `icon` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders an optional icon in a tinted tile at the top-right corner to visually reinforce the metric's meaning.
- `delta` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Shows the trend change beside the value, such as "+12.5%" or "-3%", to convey movement over a period.
- `deltaDirection` тАФ `"up" | "down" | "flat"` тАФ Forces the delta's direction and color (up, down, or flat); otherwise it is inferred from a leading minus sign.
- `helpText` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders muted context after the delta, such as "vs last month", to clarify the comparison period.
- `plain` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Strips the card chrome (border, background, and padding) so the stat blends into an existing surface.

```jsx
import { Stat } from "twico-ui";

<Stat
  label="Revenue"
  value="$48,200"
  delta="+12.5%"
  helpText="vs last month"
/>
<Stat
  label="Churn"
  value="2.1%"
  delta="-0.4%"
  deltaDirection="up"
  helpText="improved"
/>
```

#### Table

A data table with a styled header, hover and zebra-striped rows, client-side click-to-sort headers, custom cell renderers, and selected-row highlighting. Use it to display structured rows of data when you need lightweight sorting and per-column rendering or alignment.

`import { Table } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `columns` тАФ `TableColumn<T>[]` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Defines each column with key, header, optional align, width, sortable flag, and a custom render function for cell content.
- `hover` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Highlights the row under the cursor on mouse-over, on by default to aid scanning and pointing at dense rows.
- `striped` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Applies zebra striping to alternating rows when enabled, improving readability across wide tables of similar-looking data.
- `size` тАФ `"sm" | "md"` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Controls cell padding density, choosing sm for compact rows or md for the default roomier spacing.
- `sortable` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Enables client-side click-to-sort headers when true, letting users reorder rows by any column marked sortable.
- `rowKey` тАФ `(row: T, index: number) => string | number` тАФ Returns a stable unique key for each row from its data and index, defaulting to the row index.
- `selectedKeys` тАФ `Array<string | number>` тАФ Lists the keys of rows to render with selected-row highlighting, useful for marking chosen or active records.
- `sort` тАФ `TableSort | null` тАФ Controlled sort state тАФ pair with onSortChange (null = unsorted).
- `defaultSort` тАФ `TableSort` ┬╖ default `{ key: null, dir: "asc" }` тАФ Initial sort when uncontrolled.
- `onSortChange` тАФ `(sort: TableSort) => void` тАФ Fired when a sortable header is clicked with the next sort state.
- `stickyHeader` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Pin the header to the top while the body scrolls (use with maxHeight or an enclosing scroll container).
- `maxHeight` тАФ `number | string` тАФ Cap the table height and give it its own vertical scroll area (CSS length or number of px); enables stickyHeader to shine.
- `loading` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Renders shimmering skeleton rows instead of data and sets aria-busy, avoiding a flash-of-empty during fetches.
- `loadingRows` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `8` тАФ Number of skeleton rows to show while loading is true.
- `emptyMessage` тАФ `React.ReactNode` ┬╖ default `"No data"` тАФ Rendered as a single full-width row when rows is empty instead of a bare header over a blank body.
- `ariaLabel` тАФ `string` тАФ Accessible name applied to the <table> itself (not the wrapper div); a raw aria-label prop takes precedence over it.
- `caption` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders a visually-hidden <caption> naming the table for assistive technology.
- `rows` тАФ `T[]` тАФ Row objects.
- `field (column)` тАФ `string` тАФ Alias for key (Datatable vocabulary); key wins when both are set.
- `headerName (column)` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Alias for header (Datatable vocabulary); header wins when both are set.
- `renderCell (column)` тАФ `(value: any, row: T, index: number) => React.ReactNode` тАФ Alias for render (Datatable vocabulary); render wins when both are set.

```jsx
import { Table } from "twico-ui";

const users = [
  { id: 1, name: "Ava Stone", role: "Admin", status: "Active", mrr: 480 },
  { id: 2, name: "Liam Reed", role: "Editor", status: "Invited", mrr: 120 },
  { id: 3, name: "Noah Park", role: "Viewer", status: "Active", mrr: 60 },
];

<Table
  sortable
  striped
  rowKey={(r) => r.id}
  columns={[
    { field: "name", headerName: "Name" },
    { field: "role", headerName: "Role" },
    { field: "status", headerName: "Status" },
    { field: "mrr", headerName: "MRR", align: "right" },
  ]}
  rows={users}
/>
```

#### Tag

Tag is a removable chip for displaying filters, selected items, or keywords. Use it when you need compact, dismissible labels with an optional leading icon and an optional remove (├Ч) button.

`import { Tag } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `onRemove` тАФ `(e: React.MouseEvent<HTMLButtonElement>) => void` тАФ Renders the remove (├Ч) button and handles its click, letting users dismiss the tag as a filter or selection.
- `variant` тАФ `"soft" | "solid" | "outline"` ┬╖ default `"soft"` тАФ Chooses the fill style between soft, solid, and outline to control how prominently the tag reads.
- `tone` тАФ `"neutral" | "primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info"` ┬╖ default `"neutral"` тАФ Sets the semantic color from six options like success or danger; defaults to neutral (the classic chip).
- `leftIcon` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders an optional leading icon before the label, useful for signaling the tag's category or status.
- `children` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Provides the tag's label content, typically the filter name, selected item, or keyword being displayed.

```jsx
import { Tag } from "twico-ui";

<Tag onRemove={() => {}}>React</Tag>
<Tag leftIcon={<span>#</span>}>design-system</Tag>
<Tag>read-only</Tag>
```

#### Timeline

Timeline renders a vertical list of events as node dots connected by a rail, each showing a title, optional timestamp, and description. Use it for activity feeds, order tracking, or history views.

`import { Timeline } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `items` тАФ `TimelineItem[] тАФ { title, time?, description?, icon?, tone? }` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Array of events to render as connected node dots, each with a title, optional time, description, icon, and primary/success/warning/danger tone.
- `className` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `""` тАФ Extra CSS class appended to the root <ul> so you can layer custom spacing or styling onto the timeline.
- `tone (TimelineItem)` тАФ `"primary" | "info" | "success" | "warning" | "danger"` тАФ Added `info` value to the per-item node tone union; maps the dot to var(--color-info) with var(--color-info-fg) icon color.

```jsx
import { Timeline } from "twico-ui";

<Timeline
  items={[
    { title: "Order placed", time: "9:41 AM", description: "We received your order.", tone: "primary" },
    { title: "Shipped", time: "2:10 PM", description: "Left the warehouse.", tone: "success" },
    { title: "Out for delivery", time: "Tomorrow" },
  ]}
/>
```

#### Treemap

A dependency-free SVG treemap that partitions a rectangle into tiles sized in proportion to a flat list of values, using a squarified layout that keeps tile aspect ratios near 1:1. Reach for it to show the relative weight of parts within a whole тАФ spend by category, storage by bucket тАФ where a pie would crowd or a bar chart would lose the sense of area.

`import { Treemap } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `data` тАФ `TreemapDatum[]` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Tiles to lay out; each has a label, a non-negative value driving its area, and an optional color override.
- `showValues` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Draw the formatted value under the label inside tiles tall enough to fit both lines of text.
- `valueFormat` тАФ `(value: number) => string` ┬╖ default `toLocaleString` тАФ Formatter applied to the tooltip, the in-tile value, and the hidden data-table cells.
- `height` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `300` тАФ Pixel height, which also sets the SVG's aspect ratio; the width fills the container.
- `colors` тАФ `string[]` тАФ Tile colors cycled by index; defaults to the built-in token palette, and a datum's own color wins.
- `gap` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `2` тАФ Gap in pixels inset between adjacent tiles so their boundaries read clearly.
- `ariaLabel` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"treemap"` тАФ Accessible name for the chart's role="img" SVG; also accepts aria-label, with values exposed via the data table.
- `tableFallback` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Render a visually-hidden data table of value and share as an accessible text alternative (WCAG 1.1.1).
- `caption` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Caption for the hidden data table; defaults to the chart's accessible label when omitted.

```jsx
import { Treemap } from "twico-ui";

<Treemap
  ariaLabel="Cloud spend by service"
  valueFormat={(v) => `$${v.toLocaleString()}`}
  data={[
    { label: "Compute", value: 4200 },
    { label: "Storage", value: 2600 },
    { label: "Network", value: 1800 },
    { label: "Other", value: 700, color: "var(--slate-500)" },
  ]}
/>
```

### Navigation

#### Accordion

Accordion renders vertically stacked, expandable disclosure panels with a smooth open/close height animation. Use it to organize content like FAQs or settings into collapsible sections, optionally allowing several panels open at once.

`import { Accordion } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `items` тАФ `AccordionItem[]` ┬╖ **required** тАФ The array of disclosure panels to render, each an { value, label, content, icon? } object keyed by its unique value.
- `multiple` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ When true, lets several panels stay open simultaneously instead of collapsing others as each new panel expands.
- `defaultOpen` тАФ `string[]` ┬╖ default `[]` тАФ The list of item values that should start expanded on first render, useful for surfacing key sections by default.
- `open` тАФ `string[]` тАФ Controlled open panel values; pair with onOpenChange. When set, internal state is bypassed and defaultOpen is ignored.
- `onOpenChange` тАФ `(values: string[]) => void` тАФ Called with the next open values whenever a panel trigger is toggled (fires in both controlled and uncontrolled modes).

```jsx
import { Accordion } from "twico-ui";

<Accordion
  multiple
  defaultOpen={["a"]}
  items={[
    { value: "a", label: "Is Twico UI free?", content: "Yes тАФ MIT licensed, forever." },
    { value: "b", label: "Does it support dark mode?", content: "Out of the box." },
  ]}
/>
```

#### Breadcrumb

Breadcrumb renders a navigation trail to the current page, with optional per-item icons and a custom separator. Use it to show hierarchy and let users jump back up the path; it can collapse the middle of long trails into a "тАж" that expands on click.

`import { Breadcrumb } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `items` тАФ `BreadcrumbItem[]` ┬╖ **required** тАФ The ordered array of trail entries ({ label, href?, icon?, onClick? }), where the last item renders as the current, non-linked page.
- `separator` тАФ `React.ReactNode` ┬╖ default `<ChevronSep />` тАФ The node drawn between adjacent items, defaulting to a chevron but replaceable with any custom divider such as a slash.
- `maxItems` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `0` тАФ Collapses the middle of the trail into an expandable ellipsis once the item count exceeds this number, with 0 disabling collapsing.

```jsx
import { Breadcrumb } from "twico-ui";

<Breadcrumb
  items={[
    { label: "Home", href: "/" },
    { label: "Projects", href: "/projects" },
    { label: "Twico UI" },
  ]}
/>

<Breadcrumb
  maxItems={3}
  items={[
    { label: "Home", href: "/" },
    { label: "Workspace", href: "/workspace" },
    { label: "Projects", href: "/workspace/projects" },
    { label: "Twico UI", href: "/workspace/projects/twico" },
    { label: "Settings" },
  ]}
/>
```

#### Navbar

Navbar is a top application bar with a left brand/logo slot, center inline navigation links (hidden on small screens), and a right-aligned actions slot. Use it as the primary site or app header; it is sticky and translucent by default.

`import { Navbar } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `brand` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders the brand or logo node in the navbar's left slot, typically a wordmark or icon linking home.
- `links` тАФ `NavbarLink[]` ┬╖ default `[]` тАФ Inline navigation items shown centered, each with a label and optional href, icon, active state, or onClick.
- `actions` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Right-aligned content such as buttons, a search field, or an avatar for account and primary actions.
- `sticky` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Pins the navbar to the top of the viewport with a translucent blur as the page scrolls; on by default.
- `onMenuClick` тАФ `() => void` тАФ Fires when the hamburger button (shown only on small screens) is clicked, typically to open a mobile drawer.

```jsx
import { Navbar } from "twico-ui";

<Navbar
  brand={<>Twico <span style={{ color: "var(--color-primary)" }}>UI</span></>}
  links={[
    { label: "Dashboard", active: true },
    { label: "Projects" },
    { label: "Team" },
  ]}
  actions={<button>Sign in</button>}
/>
```

#### Sidebar

Sidebar is a collapsible side-navigation panel with a brand header, grouped nav items (icons, labels, badges, or uppercase section headings), an optional footer, and a collapse toggle that switches to an icon-only rail. Use it as the primary app navigation in a fixed-height layout.

`import { Sidebar } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `items` тАФ `SidebarItem[]` ┬╖ **required** тАФ The navigation entries, each a link with label, icon, href, active, onClick, and badge, or a { section } heading.
- `brand` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Brand or logo content shown in the header, with its text hidden when the sidebar collapses to the icon rail.
- `footer` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Content pinned above the collapse toggle, such as a user row, anchored to the bottom of the panel.
- `collapsed` тАФ `boolean` тАФ Controlled collapsed state, letting the parent toggle the sidebar between full width and the icon-only rail.
- `defaultCollapsed` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Initial collapsed state for uncontrolled usage, defaulting to false so the sidebar starts fully expanded.
- `collapsible` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Shows the collapse toggle button when true (the default), allowing users to switch to the icon-only rail.
- `onCollapsedChange` тАФ `(collapsed: boolean) => void` тАФ Fires with the new boolean state whenever the collapse toggle is activated, useful for persisting the preference.
- `overlay` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Renders the sidebar as an off-canvas drawer instead of an in-flow rail: a fixed slide-over panel behind a dismissable backdrop, portaled to the body, with a focus trap, Escape-to-close, and scroll lock.
- `open` тАФ `boolean` тАФ Controlled open state of the overlay drawer (only meaningful when overlay is set); the parent owns visibility.
- `defaultOpen` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Initial open state of the overlay drawer for uncontrolled usage, defaulting to closed.
- `onOpenChange` тАФ `(open: boolean) => void` тАФ Fires when the overlay drawer requests open or close (backdrop click or Escape); wire it to your open state.
- `navLabel` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"Main"` тАФ Accessible name for the inner nav landmark and the overlay dialog, so assistive tech can distinguish multiple navigations.

```jsx
import { Sidebar } from "twico-ui";

const HomeIcon = () => (<svg width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="2" strokeLinecap="round"><path d="M3 11l9-8 9 8M5 10v10h14V10" /></svg>);
const MailIcon = () => (<svg width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="2" strokeLinecap="round"><rect x="3" y="5" width="18" height="14" rx="2" /><path d="m3 7 9 6 9-6" /></svg>);
const CogIcon = () => (<svg width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="2" strokeLinecap="round"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="3" /><path d="M12 3v3M12 18v3M3 12h3M18 12h3" /></svg>);

<Sidebar
  brand={<>Twico <span style={{ color: "var(--color-primary)" }}>UI</span></>}
  items={[
    { section: "Main" },
    { label: "Dashboard", icon: <HomeIcon />, active: true },
    { label: "Inbox", icon: <MailIcon />, badge: 4 },
    { section: "Account" },
    { label: "Settings", icon: <CogIcon /> },
  ]}
  footer={<span style={{ fontSize: "var(--text-xs)", color: "var(--color-text-muted)" }}>v1.0.0</span>}
  defaultCollapsed={false}
/>
```

#### Stepper

Stepper is a multi-step progress indicator for wizards and checkout flows: steps before the active index show a check, the active step is ringed, and later steps are muted. Use it to show users where they are in a sequential, multi-step process, either horizontally or vertically.

`import { Stepper } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `steps` тАФ `Step[]  ({ title, description?, icon?, error? })` ┬╖ **required** тАФ The ordered array of step objects to render, each with a title and optional description, icon, and error flag.
- `tone` тАФ `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` ┬╖ default `"primary"` тАФ Sets the active/completed step marker and connector accent color from six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `active` тАФ `number` тАФ Controlled zero-based index of the current step (pair with onStepClick); earlier steps render complete, later steps upcoming.
- `defaultActive` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `0` тАФ Uncontrolled initial step index; the Stepper then advances itself when a clickable step is clicked.
- `orientation` тАФ `"horizontal" | "vertical"` ┬╖ default `"horizontal"` тАФ Lays the stepper out horizontally (default) or vertically to fit wide toolbars or narrow sidebars.
- `clickable` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Lets users click completed or active steps to navigate back, enabling jump-around movement through the flow.
- `onStepClick` тАФ `(index: number) => void` тАФ Callback fired with the clicked step's index when steps are clickable, letting you drive navigation.

```jsx
import { Stepper } from "twico-ui";

const [active, setActive] = useState(2);

<Stepper
  active={1}
  steps={[
    { title: "Account", description: "Your details" },
    { title: "Payment", description: "Billing info" },
    { title: "Confirm" },
  ]}
/>

<Stepper
  orientation="vertical"
  active={active}
  clickable
  onStepClick={setActive}
  steps={[
    { title: "Account", description: "Your details" },
    { title: "Payment", description: "Billing info" },
    { title: "Confirm" },
  ]}
/>
```

#### Tabs

Tabbed navigation with a sliding active indicator, available as a line or pill variant. Use it to switch between related views or sections, either controlled (value + onChange) or uncontrolled (defaultValue).

`import { Tabs } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `items` тАФ `TabItem[]` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Defines the tabs to render, each carrying a value, label, and optional icon, count badge, and content.
- `value` тАФ `string` тАФ Sets the active tab value for controlled usage, kept in sync by handling onChange yourself.
- `defaultValue` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `items[0]?.value` тАФ Sets the initially active tab for uncontrolled usage, defaulting to the first item's value when omitted.
- `onChange` тАФ `(value: string) => void` тАФ Fires with the newly selected tab value whenever the user switches tabs, used to drive controlled state.
- `variant` тАФ `"line" | "pill"` ┬╖ default `"line"` тАФ Selects the visual style, rendering either a line underline indicator or a pill-shaped background behind the active tab.
- `tone` тАФ `"primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral"` ┬╖ default `"primary"` тАФ Sets the active-indicator and active-label accent color from six semantic intents; defaults to primary.
- `orientation` тАФ `"horizontal" | "vertical"` ┬╖ default `"horizontal"` тАФ Layout axis. Vertical stacks the tablist with the active indicator on its trailing edge and the panel beside it; ArrowUp/ArrowDown navigate between tabs (Home/End still jump to ends).

```jsx
import { Tabs } from "twico-ui";

<Tabs
  variant="line"
  defaultValue="overview"
  items={[
    { value: "overview", label: "Overview", content: <p>Overview panel</p> },
    { value: "activity", label: "Activity", count: 12, content: <p>Activity panel</p> },
    { value: "settings", label: "Settings", content: <p>Settings panel</p> },
  ]}
/>
```

#### TreeView
