TreeView renders a hierarchical, expand/collapse tree with indentation, icons, badges, and a selectable row. Use it for file explorers and nested navigation where items have parent/child relationships.

`import { TreeView } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `defaultExpanded` тАФ `string[]` ┬╖ default `[]` тАФ Node ids that start expanded on first render, letting you reveal specific branches of the tree by default.
- `defaultSelectedId` тАФ `string | null` ┬╖ default `null` тАФ Selected node id on first render in uncontrolled mode; the tree then manages selection itself.
- `selectedId` тАФ `string | null` тАФ Controlled id of the currently selected node; omit it to let the component manage selection internally on its own.
- `onSelect` тАФ `(id: string, node: TreeNode) => void` тАФ Fired when a row is clicked, id first (the full node is the second argument).
- `expanded` тАФ `string[]` тАФ Controlled expanded node ids (pair with `onExpandedChange`).
- `onExpandedChange` тАФ `(ids: string[]) => void` тАФ Fired with the next expanded ids whenever a row expands or collapses.
- `items` тАФ `TreeNode[]` ┬╖ default `[]` тАФ Tree nodes.

```jsx
import { TreeView } from "twico-ui";

<TreeView
  defaultExpanded={["src"]}
  onSelect={(id) => open(id)}
  items={[
    { id: "src", label: "src", children: [
      { id: "app", label: "App.tsx" },
      { id: "comp", label: "components", children: [
        { id: "btn", label: "Button.tsx", badge: 3 },
      ]},
    ]},
  ]}
/>
```

### Overlay

#### CommandPalette

A тМШK-style command palette overlay with searchable, grouped commands and full keyboard navigation (тЖС/тЖУ, Enter, Esc). Use it to give users a fast, central way to run actions or jump around the app, typically opened on Cmd/Ctrl+K.

`import { CommandPalette } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `open` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Controls whether the palette overlay is visible; you own this state and typically toggle it on Cmd/Ctrl+K.
- `onClose` тАФ `() => void` тАФ Fires when the palette should dismiss, such as on Esc, an overlay click, or after a command is selected.
- `commands` тАФ `Command[]` ┬╖ **required** тАФ The list of Command objects to search, group, and render, each typically defining a label, action, and optional group.
- `placeholder` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"Type a command or searchтАж"` тАФ Sets the search input's placeholder text, defaulting to "Type a command or searchтАж" to hint at the expected query.
- `emptyText` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"No results found"` тАФ The message shown when no command matches the current query, defaulting to "No results found".
- `onClick` тАФ `() => void` тАФ Alias for onSelect, matching the other item-array APIs (Menu, List, Sidebar, ...) тАФ used when onSelect is absent; palette closes after it runs.

```jsx
import { CommandPalette } from "twico-ui";

const [open, setOpen] = React.useState(false);
React.useEffect(() => {
  const h = (e) => {
    if ((e.metaKey || e.ctrlKey) && e.key === "k") { e.preventDefault(); setOpen(true); }
  };
  window.addEventListener("keydown", h);
  return () => window.removeEventListener("keydown", h);
}, []);

<CommandPalette
  open={open}
  onClose={() => setOpen(false)}
  commands={[
    { group: "Navigation", label: "Go to Dashboard", shortcut: "G D", onSelect: () => {} },
    { group: "Actions", label: "New project", keywords: "create add", onSelect: () => {} },
  ]}
/>
```

#### Dialog

Dialog is a modal overlay with a scrim, pop-in animation, focus trapping, Esc-to-close, and backdrop-close. Use it to interrupt the user for confirmations, forms, or detail views that require attention before continuing.

`import { Dialog } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `open` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Controls whether the modal is visible; the dialog renders null when false, so drive it from state.
- `onClose` тАФ `() => void` тАФ Called when the dialog requests to close via Esc, a backdrop click, or the close button.
- `title` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Heading content rendered in the dialog header to announce the modal's purpose to the user.
- `description` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Secondary supporting text shown beneath the title to give context for the dialog's content or task.
- `footer` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Footer region for pinned actions, typically right-aligned confirm and cancel buttons below the body.
- `size` тАФ `"sm" | "md" | "lg" | "full"` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Adds a new "full" option: a near-fullscreen panel that fills the viewport minus a small margin (existing sm/md/lg unchanged).
- `closeOnBackdrop` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Closes the dialog when the surrounding scrim is clicked, enabled by default; disable for required interactions.
- `children` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Main body content of the dialog, such as forms, messages, or detail views requiring the user's attention.
- `scrollBody` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Keep the header and footer fixed and scroll only the body, so long content does not overflow the viewport.

```jsx
import { Dialog, Button } from "twico-ui";

const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);

<Button tone="danger" onClick={() => setOpen(true)}>Delete project</Button>

<Dialog
  open={open}
  onClose={() => setOpen(false)}
  title="Delete project?"
  description="This action cannot be undone."
  footer={
    <>
      <Button variant="ghost" onClick={() => setOpen(false)}>Cancel</Button>
      <Button tone="danger" onClick={() => setOpen(false)}>Delete</Button>
    </>
  }
>
  The project and all its data will be permanently removed.
</Dialog>
```

#### Drawer

Drawer is a slide-in overlay panel that anchors to any screen edge with a scrim, Esc-to-close, focus trapping, and a header/body/footer layout. Use it for nav menus, filters, detail views, or carts that should appear over the current page; drive it with the open and onClose props.

`import { Drawer } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `open` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Controls whether the panel is visible; the drawer renders nothing when false, so drive it from state.
- `onClose` тАФ `() => void` тАФ Called when the drawer requests to close via Esc, a backdrop click, or the close button.
- `side` тАФ `"left" | "right" | "top" | "bottom"` ┬╖ default `"right"` тАФ Screen edge the panel anchors to and slides in from, defaulting to right (left, right, top, or bottom).
- `title` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Heading content rendered in the drawer header to announce the panel's purpose to the user.
- `description` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Secondary supporting text shown under the title to give context for the drawer's content or task.
- `footer` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Footer region for pinned actions, typically right-aligned buttons displayed below the drawer body.
- `closeOnBackdrop` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `true` тАФ Closes the drawer when the backdrop scrim is clicked, enabled by default; disable for required interactions.
- `children` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Body content of the drawer, such as nav menus, filters, detail views, or a cart over the page.
- `width` тАФ `"sm" | "md" | "lg" | number | string` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Panel width for side left/right тАФ preset sm/md/lg, a number (px), or a CSS length.
- `height` тАФ `"sm" | "md" | "lg" | number | string` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Panel height for side top/bottom тАФ preset sm/md/lg, a number (px), or a CSS length.

```jsx
import { Drawer } from "twico-ui";

const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);

<Drawer
  open={open}
  onClose={() => setOpen(false)}
  side="right"
  title="Filters"
  description="Narrow down the results"
  footer={<button onClick={() => setOpen(false)}>Apply</button>}
>
  ...filter controls...
</Drawer>
```

#### Menu

Menu is a portaled dropdown menu that takes a clickable trigger and an array of items supporting icons, shortcuts, separators, section headings, and danger styling. Use it for contextual action lists (e.g. an account or "more options" menu) that need to stay keyboard-navigable and never get clipped by overflow.

`import { Menu } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `trigger` тАФ `React.ReactNode` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Defines the clickable element that opens the menu, typically a Button or IconButton the dropdown anchors to.
- `items` тАФ `MenuItemDef[]` ┬╖ **required** тАФ Lists the menu entries, where each can be an action item, a separator, or a section heading.
- `align` тАФ `"start" | "end"` ┬╖ default `"start"` тАФ Aligns the menu horizontally to the trigger's start or end edge so it sits flush in tight layouts; defaults to start.
- `header` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders an optional rich node above the items, handy for surfacing user info or context at the top of the menu.
- `width` тАФ `number` тАФ Pins the menu to a fixed pixel width, otherwise it sizes to the larger of 200px and the trigger's width.
- `defaultOpen` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Open on first render in uncontrolled mode; the Menu then tracks its own open state.
- `open` тАФ `boolean` тАФ Controlled open state - pair with onOpenChange. Omit for internal (uncontrolled) state.
- `onOpenChange` тАФ `(open: boolean) => void` тАФ Called with the requested open state on trigger click, item select, Esc, or outside click.

```jsx
import { Menu, Button } from "twico-ui";

<Menu
  align="end"
  trigger={<Button>Options</Button>}
  items={[
    { label: "Account", heading: true },
    { label: "Profile", onClick: () => {}, shortcut: "тМШP" },
    { label: "Settings", onClick: () => {} },
    { separator: true },
    { label: "Sign out", danger: true, onClick: () => {} },
  ]}
/>
```

#### Popover

Popover is a click-triggered floating panel anchored to a trigger element, rendered in a portal so it is never clipped, auto-flipping near viewport edges and closing on outside-click or Esc. Use it for rich interactive content (inputs, buttons, links); for plain text hints use Tooltip instead.

`import { Popover } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `trigger` тАФ `React.ReactNode` ┬╖ **required** тАФ The clickable element the panel anchors to; clicking it toggles the popover open and closed.
- `title` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Optional bold heading rendered at the top of the panel to introduce its interactive content.
- `placement` тАФ `"top" | "bottom" | "left" | "right"` ┬╖ default `"bottom"` тАФ Which side of the trigger the panel opens toward (top, bottom, left, or right), auto-flipping near viewport edges.
- `align` тАФ `"start" | "center" | "end"` ┬╖ default `"center"` тАФ Cross-axis alignment (start, center, or end) of the panel relative to the trigger for top and bottom placements.
- `width` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `240` тАФ Fixed width of the floating panel in pixels, controlling how much interactive content fits on each line.
- `children` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ The rich interactive content rendered inside the panel, such as inputs, buttons, or links.
- `defaultOpen` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Open on first render in uncontrolled mode; the Popover then tracks its own open state.
- `open` тАФ `boolean` тАФ Controlled open state - pair with onOpenChange. Omit for internal (uncontrolled) state.
- `onOpenChange` тАФ `(open: boolean) => void` тАФ Called with the requested open state on trigger click, Esc, or outside click.

```jsx
import { Popover, Button, Input } from "twico-ui";

<Popover
  trigger={<Button variant="outline">Share</Button>}
  title="Share link"
  placement="bottom"
>
  <Input defaultValue="https://twico.dev/x" />
  <Button fullWidth>Copy link</Button>
</Popover>
```

#### Tooltip

Tooltip is a small hover/focus overlay that wraps a single trigger element and shows a label on interaction. Use it to surface short, supplementary hints (like icon-button descriptions) without cluttering the UI.

`import { Tooltip } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `label` тАФ `React.ReactNode` ┬╖ **required** тАФ The short hint text or content revealed on hover or focus, ideal for describing icon buttons without cluttering the layout.
- `placement` тАФ `"top" | "bottom" | "left" | "right"` ┬╖ default `"top"` тАФ Chooses which side of the trigger the tooltip appears on, one of top, bottom, left, or right (default top).
- `delay` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `120` тАФ Open delay in milliseconds before the tooltip appears (default 120), preventing flicker as the pointer passes over triggers.
- `children` тАФ `React.ReactNode` ┬╖ **required** тАФ The single trigger element the tooltip wraps and attaches its hover and focus listeners to.

```jsx
import { Tooltip } from "twico-ui";

<Tooltip label="Copy to clipboard" placement="top">
  <button aria-label="Copy">Copy</button>
</Tooltip>
```

### Feedback

#### Alert

Alert is an inline message banner with four semantic tones (info, success, warning, danger), an optional bold title, a default or custom icon, and an optional dismiss button. Use it to surface contextual feedback inline within a page, such as success confirmations, warnings, or errors.

`import { Alert } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `tone` тАФ `"info" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "primary" | "neutral"` ┬╖ default `"info"` тАФ Sets the semantic color from six intents (info, success, warning, danger, primary, neutral); defaults to info.
- `variant` тАФ `"soft" | "solid" | "outline"` ┬╖ default `"soft"` тАФ Chooses the fill style between soft (tinted), solid (filled), and outline; defaults to soft.
- `title` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders a bold heading line above the body, useful for summarizing the alert before its supporting detail.
- `icon` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Overrides the default tone icon with your own node when you need a more specific or branded visual cue.
- `onClose` тАФ `() => void` тАФ When provided, shows a dismiss button and runs this callback so the alert can be closed by the user.
- `children` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ The main body content of the alert, typically the descriptive message shown beneath the optional title.

```jsx
import { Alert } from "twico-ui";

<Alert tone="success" title="Saved" onClose={() => {}}>
  Your changes are live.
</Alert>

<Alert tone="warning">
  Your trial ends in 3 days.
</Alert>
```

#### EmptyState

EmptyState is a zero-data placeholder that centers an optional icon tile, title, description, and an actions row for a call-to-action. Use it when a list, table, inbox, or panel has no content yet, or as a dashed drop-target placeholder.

`import { EmptyState } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `icon` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders an illustrative icon inside a tinted tile above the title, signalling visually why the area has no content yet.
- `title` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Sets the centered headline summarising the empty condition, such as "No results" or "Your inbox is empty".
- `description` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Provides supporting copy beneath the title that explains the empty state and hints at how to fill it.
- `actions` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Renders a row of call-to-action buttons below the text so users can immediately add content or take the next step.
- `bordered` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Adds a dashed border around the placeholder when true, ideal for drop targets or visually distinct empty regions.
- `border` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Add a dashed border (good for drop targets / placeholders); preferred alias for bordered.

```jsx
import { EmptyState, Button } from "twico-ui";

<EmptyState
  icon={<span style={{ fontSize: 28 }}>ЁЯУн</span>}
  title="No messages yet"
  description="When someone sends you a message, it'll show up here."
  actions={<Button>Compose</Button>}
/>
```

#### Progress

Progress is a linear progress bar that can be determinate (driven by a value) or indeterminate, with semantic tones. Use it to show task completion, upload/download progress, or ongoing background work.

`import { Progress } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `value` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `0` тАФ Current progress amount that drives how much of the bar is filled, interpreted relative to max.
- `max` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `100` тАФ Upper bound the bar represents, so value divided by max determines the filled percentage.
- `tone` тАФ `"primary" | "info" | "success" | "warning" | "danger"` ┬╖ default `"primary"` тАФ Added `info` value to the existing tone union; maps the bar fill to var(--color-info).
- `size` тАФ `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Thickness of the bar, sm, md, or lg, to match the visual weight of the surrounding content.
- `indeterminate` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Switches to a continuously animated bar that ignores value, signaling work of unknown duration.
- `showLabel` тАФ `boolean` ┬╖ default `false` тАФ Displays a percentage label above the bar so users can read the exact completion at a glance.

```jsx
import { Progress } from "twico-ui";

<Progress value={64} showLabel />
<Progress value={90} tone="success" />
<Progress value={45} tone="warning" size="lg" />
<Progress indeterminate />
```

#### Skeleton

Skeleton is a shimmering placeholder shown while content loads. Use it to reserve layout space and signal loading for text, avatars, or image/card blocks before real data arrives.

`import { Skeleton } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `variant` тАФ `"text" | "circle" | "rect"` ┬╖ default `"text"` тАФ Chooses the placeholder shape: text for lines, circle for avatars, or rect for image and card blocks, defaulting to text.
- `width` тАФ `string | number` тАФ Sets the placeholder width as a CSS string or number, such as "120px" or 60, to match expected content.
- `height` тАФ `string | number` тАФ Sets the placeholder height as a CSS string or number to reserve the vertical space the real content will occupy.
- `lines` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `1` тАФ For the text variant, the number of lines to render, defaulting to 1, with the last line shortened for realism.

```jsx
import { Skeleton } from "twico-ui";

<Skeleton variant="circle" width={40} height={40} />
<Skeleton variant="text" lines={3} />
<Skeleton variant="rect" height={120} />
```

#### Spinner

An indeterminate loading spinner that signals an ongoing background operation. Use it when content or an action is loading and there's no measurable progress to show, such as inside buttons or over panels.

`import { Spinner } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `size` тАФ `"sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl"` ┬╖ default `"md"` тАФ Sets the spinner diameter (sm, md, lg, or xl) so it fits its context, from inside a button to over a panel.
- `tone` тАФ `"current" | "primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral" | "white"` ┬╖ default `"current"` тАФ Deprecated alias for color.
- `label` тАФ `string` ┬╖ default `"Loading"` тАФ Accessible name exposed via aria-label so screen readers announce the loading state, defaulting to "Loading".
- `color` тАФ `"current" | "primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "neutral" | "white"` ┬╖ default `"current"` тАФ Sets the spinner color; defaults to currentColor so it adapts inside buttons and themed contexts.

```jsx
import { Spinner } from "twico-ui";

<Spinner />
<Spinner size="lg" />
<Spinner tone="white" /> {/* on a colored button/background */}
```

#### Toast

Toast is a transient notification card with a tone accent, optional title/icon, and a dismiss button. Use it to surface brief feedback (success, warning, error, info) by managing a list of toasts in state and rendering them inside a fixed-position ToastViewport.

`import { Toast, ToastViewport } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `tone` тАФ `"default" | "neutral" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info"` ┬╖ default `"default"` тАФ "neutral" is now accepted as an alias for "default" (aligns with Badge's tone vocabulary). No new standalone props were added.
- `title` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Bold heading text shown above the body content to summarize what the toast is reporting at a glance.
- `icon` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Custom leading icon that overrides the default tone icon when you want a more specific visual marker.
- `onClose` тАФ `() => void` тАФ Callback invoked on dismissal; providing it also reveals the close button so users can manually remove the toast.
- `duration` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `4500` тАФ Milliseconds before the toast auto-dismisses and calls onClose (default 4500); the countdown pauses on hover or focus, and 0 or Infinity keeps it open.
- `children` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Body or description content rendered beneath the title to give users the detailed message of the notification.
- `limit (ToastViewport)` тАФ `number` тАФ Caps how many toasts the viewport shows at once, keeping the most recent and dropping older ones so bursts never flood the screen.

```jsx
import { Toast, ToastViewport } from "twico-ui";

const [toasts, setToasts] = useState([]);
const push = (t) => setToasts((s) => [...s, { id: Date.now(), ...t }]);
const remove = (id) => setToasts((s) => s.filter((t) => t.id !== id));

{/* limit caps what's shown; each toast auto-dismisses after ~4.5s */}
<ToastViewport limit={4}>
  {toasts.map((t) => (
    <Toast
      key={t.id}
      tone={t.tone}
      title={t.title}
      onClose={() => remove(t.id)}
    >
      {t.body}
    </Toast>
  ))}
</ToastViewport>
```

#### ToastProvider

ToastProvider drops a ready-made toast manager into your tree: it owns the toast list and renders a single ToastViewport, so you no longer hand-manage an array in state. Anywhere beneath it, call const { toast } = useToast() and fire toast.success("Saved") or toast.error({ title, description }); each toast auto-dismisses on the shared timer.

`import { ToastProvider, useToast } from "twico-ui"`

Props:
- `children` тАФ `React.ReactNode` тАФ Your app тАФ anything under the provider can call useToast().
- `limit` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `4` тАФ Maximum toasts shown at once (the most recent are kept).
- `duration` тАФ `number` ┬╖ default `4500` тАФ Default auto-dismiss duration (ms) for toasts that don't set their own.
- `toast() (useToast)` тАФ `(opts) => number` тАФ Push a toast; has tone helpers toast.success/warning/danger/error/info(title, options?).
- `dismiss / clear (useToast)` тАФ `(id) => void / () => void` тАФ Remove one toast by id, or clear them all.

```jsx
import { ToastProvider, useToast, Button } from "twico-ui";

function App() {
  return (
    <ToastProvider limit={4}>
      <Page />
    </ToastProvider>
  );
}

function Page() {
  const { toast } = useToast();
  return (
    <Button onClick={() => toast.success("Saved")}>Save</Button>
  );
}
```

## Hooks (25)

### useIsomorphicLayoutEffect

SSR-safe `useLayoutEffect` (falls back to `useEffect` on the server).

```ts
export const useIsomorphicLayoutEffect: typeof React.useLayoutEffect;
```

### useMounted

`true` once the component has mounted on the client тАФ for SSR-safe rendering.

```ts
export function useMounted(): boolean;
```

### usePrevious

The value from the previous render.

```ts
export function usePrevious<T>(value: T): T | undefined;
```

### useToggle

Boolean state with a stable toggle. `toggle()` flips; `toggle(true)` sets.

```ts
export function useToggle( initial?: boolean ): [boolean, (next?: boolean) => void, React.Dispatch<React.SetStateAction<boolean>>];
```

### useDisclosure

Open/close state for dialogs, drawers, menus, popovers.

```ts
export function useDisclosure(initial?: boolean): { open: boolean; onOpen: () => void; onClose: () => void; onToggle: () => void; setOpen: React.Dispatch<React.SetStateAction<boolean>>; };
```

### useControllableState

Controlled/uncontrolled state тАФ the pattern every form component needs. Returns `[value, setValue, isControlled]`; the third element reports whether a `value` prop is currently driving the state. In development it warns once if a component switches between controlled and uncontrolled.

```ts
export function useControllableState<T>(options?: { value?: T; defaultValue?: T; onChange?: (value: T) => void; }): [T, (next: T | ((prev: T) => T)) => void, boolean];
```

### useMediaQuery

Reactively match a media query. Returns `defaultValue` on the server and the first client render, then syncs after mount тАФ so hydration never mismatches.

```ts
export function useMediaQuery(query: string, options?: UseMediaQueryOptions): boolean;
```

### usePrefersReducedMotion

`true` when the user requests reduced motion.

```ts
export function usePrefersReducedMotion(): boolean;
```

### useColorScheme

Light/dark theme state synced to `<html>` and persisted to localStorage.

```ts
export function useColorScheme(options?: UseColorSchemeOptions): { theme: "light" | "dark"; setTheme: (theme: "light" | "dark") => void; toggle: () => void; isDark: boolean; };
```

### useEventListener

Attach an event listener (window by default, or a ref/element) with cleanup.

```ts
export function useEventListener( eventName: string, handler: (event: any) => void, element?: React.RefObject<any | null> | EventTarget | null, options?: boolean | AddEventListenerOptions ): void;
```

### useClickOutside

Call `handler` when a pointer/touch event lands outside `ref`.

```ts
export function useClickOutside<T extends HTMLElement = HTMLElement>( ref: React.RefObject<T | null>, handler: (event: Event) => void, events?: string[] ): void;
```

### useKeyPress

Run `handler` on a keydown matching `key`, with optional modifier requirements.

```ts
export function useKeyPress( key: string | string[], handler: (event: KeyboardEvent) => void, options?: UseKeyPressOptions ): void;
```

### useLocalStorage

State persisted to localStorage (JSON-serialized), SSR-safe.

```ts
export function useLocalStorage<T>( key: string, initialValue: T ): [T, (value: T | ((prev: T) => T)) => void];
```

### useCopyToClipboard

Copy text to the clipboard with a `copied` flag that auto-resets.

```ts
export function useCopyToClipboard(timeout?: number): { copied: boolean; copy: (text: string) => Promise<boolean>; };
```

### useDebouncedValue

Debounce a value тАФ updates only after `delay` ms of no change.

```ts
export function useDebouncedValue<T>(value: T, delay?: number): T;
```

### useDebouncedCallback

A debounced version of a callback.

```ts
export function useDebouncedCallback<A extends any[]>( callback: (...args: A) => void, delay?: number ): (...args: A) => void;
```

### useInterval

Run `callback` every `delay` ms; pass `delay = null` to pause.

```ts
export function useInterval(callback: () => void, delay: number | null): void;
```

### useTimeout

Run `callback` once after `delay` ms; pass `delay = null` to cancel.

```ts
export function useTimeout(callback: () => void, delay: number | null): void;
```

### useWindowSize

The current window dimensions (0├Ч0 on the server).

```ts
export function useWindowSize(): { width: number; height: number };
```

### useHover

`true` while the pointer is over the element referenced by `ref`.

```ts
export function useHover<T extends HTMLElement = HTMLElement>(ref: React.RefObject<T | null>): boolean;
```

### useIntersectionObserver

The latest IntersectionObserver entry for the element referenced by `ref`.

```ts
export function useIntersectionObserver<T extends HTMLElement = HTMLElement>( ref: React.RefObject<T | null>, options?: IntersectionObserverInit ): IntersectionObserverEntry | null;
```

### useScrollLock

Lock body scroll while `locked` is true тАФ for modals/drawers.

```ts
export function useScrollLock(locked?: boolean): void;
```

### useId

A stable, SSR-safe unique id (optionally prefixed).

```ts
export function useId(prefix?: string): string;
```

### useFocusTrap

Trap focus inside the modal region referenced by `ref`. While `active`, focus moves inside on activate, Tab/Shift+Tab cycle within it, and (by default) focus is restored to the previously-focused element on deactivate. Escape is NOT handled тАФ that stays with the component. SSR-safe.

```ts
export function useFocusTrap<T extends HTMLElement = HTMLElement>( ref: React.RefObject<T | null>, active?: boolean, options?: UseFocusTrapOptions ): void;
```

### usePortal

Returns a stable `render(node)` that portals `node` to `document.body`, or `null` on the server (no document). Centralizes the overlay portal pattern.

```ts
export function usePortal(): (node: React.ReactNode) => React.ReactPortal | React.ReactNode | null;
```

---

_Generated by `scripts/gen-llms.mjs`. Do not edit by hand тАФ run `npm run gen:llms`. Source of truth: the component `.d.ts` files and `hooks/index.d.ts`._
